title: Reflection: forbid coding agents from running 'br close' on their own beads
description:
## Problem

During bd-1cs (first attempt, Judge-failed), the Coding Subagent ran `br close bd-1cs` itself before the Judge could review. This caused two cascading problems:

1. **DB / .jsonl divergence on retry.** `.beads/beads.db` is gitignored; `jj abandon @` does not roll back the DB. The retry attempt started against a 'closed' DB state and the .jsonl got sync'd back to closed by br's auto-flush.
2. **Bead-state commit pollution.** The close-state transition leaked into a later "Amend bd-1cs description" commit because of br's auto-flush boundary.

Subsequent beads mitigated this by emphatic 'do NOT run br close' instructions in the Coordinator's per-bead prompt. But this lives only in per-bead prompt construction — a new Coordinator instance has to rediscover the rule.

## Action

Edit `.claude/agents/coding.md` to add an explicit subsection (e.g. "## Bead state — do not touch") that states:

- The Coding Subagent MUST NOT run `br close`, `br update --status`, or any other state-mutating `br` command on its own bead.
- Only the Coordinator advances bead state, and only after the Judge passes.
- If the bead description asks the agent to update bead state, the agent should refuse and report the conflict to the Coordinator.

Optionally add a one-line reminder in the Definition of Done section of `.claude/agents/coding.md`: "Do not run `br close` — the Coordinator does that."

## Acceptance

- `.claude/agents/coding.md` updated.
- Single commit with the standard trailer.
- No code changes.

title: Resolve placeholder `pub enum Error {}` in src/lib.rs
description:
src/lib.rs:10 declares `pub enum Error {}` — an empty, uninhabited public enum with no constructors, no `Display`/`Error` impls, and no callers. It is dead surface area.

Two acceptable resolutions:
1. Remove the line entirely. Crate-level error consolidation can be designed when (or if) it is actually needed; right now each module that needs an Error type defines its own (e.g. `crate::model::Error`).
2. Replace it with a real top-level Error enum that wraps the module-level errors (e.g. `Model(crate::model::Error)`), with `From` impls and `Display` / `std::error::Error` implementations.

Recommended: option 1. The cost of premature consolidation is higher than the cost of adding it later. Steps 4-8 will reveal the actual shape needed.

Acceptance: src/lib.rs is clean of dead types. cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo nextest run all pass.

title: Read: types + impl + tests + registration (full slice, compressed)
description:
## Context

Compressed all-in-one bead for the Read tool. The pattern is now well-established (edit.rs, exec_bash.rs); shared helpers exist (`crate::builtins::path::{resolve_path, PathResolveError}`). Read is the simplest of the remaining tools — types, impl, tests, and registration land naturally as one coherent diff.

## Required reading

1. blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md (sections "Read" under behavioral spec; "Per-tool error enums" -> ReadError; "Per-tool input shapes")
2. blueprint/builtin-tools/PROMPT.md (the "Read" bullet)
3. src/builtins/edit.rs (the template -- types, helpers, Tool impl, atomic-write helper usage, test module)
4. src/builtins/path.rs (the shared `resolve_path` + `PathResolveError`)
5. ARCHITECTURE.md (the `### Read` stub line under `## Built-in Tools`)
6. src/tool.rs (Tool trait)
7. RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md (no unwrap/expect in production)

## Goal

Read tool fully shipped: types, impl, comprehensive unit tests, registration in `HarnessBuilder`, ARCHITECTURE.md filled in.

## Required deliverables

### 1. NEW file `src/builtins/read.rs`

```rust
use crate::builtins::path::{resolve_path, PathResolveError};
use crate::tool::ToolError;

#[derive(serde::Deserialize)]
struct ReadInput {
    path: String,
    start_line: Option<usize>,
    end_line: Option<usize>,
}

#[derive(serde::Serialize)]
#[serde(tag = "kind", rename_all = "snake_case")]
enum ReadError {
    FileNotFound { path: String },
    FileTooLarge { size_bytes: u64, max_bytes: u64 },
    NotUtf8 { byte_offset: usize },
    InvalidRange { start_line: usize, end_line: usize },
    InvalidPath { reason: String },
    IoError { reason: String },
}

fn err(e: ReadError) -> ToolError { /* uses unwrap_or_else like edit.rs */ }

#[derive(serde::Serialize)]
struct ReadOutput {
    content: String,
}

pub struct Read;

impl Read {
    pub fn new() -> Self { Read }
}

impl Default for Read { fn default() -> Self { Self::new() } }

// Tool impl using #[jsonrpsee::core::async_trait] matching exec_bash.rs / edit.rs.
```

### 2. Tool::call behavior

Per PLAN.md "Read" behavioral spec:

1. Resolve `path` via `resolve_path`. Map `PathResolveError::{Empty,Cwd}` to `ReadError::InvalidPath`.
2. `tokio::fs::metadata` to get file size.
   - On error: `NotFound` kind → `FileNotFound { path: resolved.display().to_string() }`; otherwise `IoError`.
3. **Size check**: const `MAX_BYTES: u64 = 64 * 1024;`. If `size > MAX_BYTES AND start_line is None AND end_line is None`, return `FileTooLarge { size_bytes, max_bytes }`. (If a line range is given, proceed regardless of size — we trust the range to bound output.)
4. `tokio::fs::read(path)` -- bytes.
5. Validate UTF-8 via `std::str::from_utf8(&bytes)`. On Err: `NotUtf8 { byte_offset: e.valid_up_to() }`.
6. Now have content as `&str`.
7. If `start_line` or `end_line` are set:
   - 1-indexed, inclusive.
   - `start_line == Some(0)` → `InvalidRange { start_line: 0, end_line }`.
   - `start_line` and `end_line` both set AND `start > end` → `InvalidRange`.
   - Compute total_lines = `content.lines().count()` (note: `.lines()` ignores trailing newline existence — for line-range purposes that's correct).
   - `start_line > total_lines` → empty content (sensible, no error).
   - `end_line > total_lines` → clamp to total_lines.
   - Apply slicing: collect lines into a Vec, take the slice, join with `\n`. **Important**: if the original content ended with `\n` AND the last slice included the last line, preserve the trailing newline in the output. (Otherwise the slice naturally has no trailing newline because `.lines()` strips it.)
8. If no range was given, return the full content as-is (trailing newline preserved naturally).
9. Return `ReadOutput { content }`.

### 3. Edge cases (from PLAN)

- Empty file: `{content: ""}`.
- File ends with `\n`: preserved in `content` (when no range or when range includes the last line).
- CRLF: preserved.
- Symlinks: followed (this is `tokio::fs::read`'s default).
- `path` empty → `InvalidPath`.

### 4. Update `src/builtins.rs`

Add `pub mod read;` and `pub use read::Read;`.

### 5. Register in `src/lib.rs`

Add `.add_tool(Arc::new(Read::new()))` alongside the existing registrations (AddTool, ExecBash, Edit). Widen the `use` import:
```rust
use crate::builtins::{AddTool, Edit, ExecBash, Read};
```
(Alphabetical.)

### 6. ARCHITECTURE.md

Replace the `*Stub -- filled in by the Read registration bead.*` line under `### Read` with a real paragraph:

```markdown
### Read

Reads a UTF-8 text file with an optional 1-indexed inclusive line range.
Input: `{path, start_line?, end_line?}`. Output: `{content: String}`. The
file is resolved (absolute or cwd-relative); if it does not exist, the
tool returns `FileNotFound { path }`. If the file exceeds 64 KiB and no
line range is provided, the tool returns `FileTooLarge { size_bytes,
max_bytes }` (a line range bypasses this cap). The file is read in full
and UTF-8 validated; non-UTF-8 returns `NotUtf8 { byte_offset }`. Range
semantics: `start_line` of 0 or `start > end` returns `InvalidRange`; a
start beyond the file is empty (not an error); an `end_line` past the
file is clamped. Trailing newlines and CRLF are preserved. Other errors:
`InvalidPath { reason }`, `IoError { reason }`.
```

### 7. Unit tests in `src/builtins/read.rs`

`#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block with at least these 9 `#[tokio::test]`s:

1. `read_full_file_happy_path` — write fixture "hello\nworld\n", read with no range, assert content matches exactly.
2. `read_empty_file_returns_empty_content` — write fixture "", assert content == "".
3. `read_file_not_found_returns_typed_error` — nonexistent path, assert `kind == "file_not_found"`.
4. `read_file_too_large_when_no_range_given` — write 65*1024 bytes of 'a', read with no range, assert `kind == "file_too_large"` with `size_bytes == 66560`, `max_bytes == 65536`.
5. `read_file_too_large_bypassed_by_line_range` — same fixture, read with `start_line: 1, end_line: 1`. Assert `Ok(value)` with content being the (full) single line.
6. `read_not_utf8_returns_byte_offset` — write `[0x48, 0x69, 0xFF, 0x80]`, assert `kind == "not_utf8"`, `byte_offset == 2`.
7. `read_invalid_range_start_zero` — fixture "a\nb", call with `start_line: 0, end_line: 1`, assert `kind == "invalid_range"`.
8. `read_invalid_range_start_gt_end` — fixture "a\nb\nc", call with `start_line: 2, end_line: 1`, assert `kind == "invalid_range"`.
9. `read_range_clamps_end_past_total` — fixture "a\nb", call with `start_line: 1, end_line: 99`, assert `Ok` with content `"a\nb"` (or `"a\nb\n"` if trailing newline preserved — match whichever your impl produces).
10. `read_start_past_total_returns_empty_content` — fixture "a\n", call with `start_line: 99, end_line: 100`, assert `Ok` with `content == ""`.
11. `read_invalid_path_empty_string` — call with `path: ""`, assert `kind == "invalid_path"`.

(10 tests if you skip the bonus; aim for 9+.)

Use the same `unique_tempdir()` helper pattern as `src/builtins/edit.rs`'s test module (you can copy it directly).

## CRITICAL constraints

- **No `unwrap()`/`expect()` in production code** (use `?`, `match`, `map_err`).
- **No new Cargo deps.**
- **All five files modified**: `src/builtins/read.rs` (A), `src/builtins.rs` (M), `src/lib.rs` (M), `ARCHITECTURE.md` (M). Possibly `src/builtins/path.rs` if you find the helper needs no changes (you shouldn't need to touch it).
- **Test count must go UP by at least 9** from the current 114 passed.
- **Commit has trailer**.

## jj workflow

1. `jj st` -- @ should be empty.
2. `jj desc -m "$(printf 'Close bd-XXX: Read tool (types + impl + tests + registration)\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>\n')"` (substitute the bead ID).
3. Make changes. Verify trailer with `jj log -r @ --no-graph -T 'description'`.

## Definition of Done

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` -- 123+ passed, 6 skipped.
- `rg "\.unwrap\(\)|\.expect\(" src/builtins/read.rs` -- all hits inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`.
- `jj diff --summary`: 4 files (read.rs A, builtins.rs M, lib.rs M, ARCHITECTURE.md M).
- Commit has trailer.

## Plan-respect note

Read uses the shared `resolve_path` helper but maps the dialect-free error to its own per-tool `ReadError::InvalidPath` — preserving per-tool error ownership while sharing the policy. The 64 KiB-without-range cap is Read's dialect; the resolution policy is portable.

title: Phase 2 Step 4: Add correlation IDs to Block::ToolCall and Block::ToolResult
description:
Reconcile Block (History event vocabulary) with ContentPart (Model input vocabulary) so that tool exchanges carry the same `tool_use_id` correlation key end-to-end. The new ContentPart::ToolUse carries an `id` and ContentPart::ToolResult carries a `tool_use_id`; the corresponding Block variants currently do not. The integration beads cannot wire tool calls cleanly until this is fixed.

Scope -- src/history.rs only (with cascade fix-ups elsewhere):

Replace the existing Block::ToolCall and Block::ToolResult variants:

```rust
ToolCall {
    id: String,
    name: String,
    arguments: serde_json::Value,
    timestamp: SystemTime,
},
ToolResult {
    tool_use_id: String,
    name: String,
    result: serde_json::Value,
    is_error: bool,
    timestamp: SystemTime,
},
```

Field rationale:
- `Block::ToolCall.id`: provider-issued tool-use identifier. Must equal the eventual `ContentPart::ToolUse.id` emitted to the Model in subsequent re-runs.
- `Block::ToolResult.tool_use_id`: correlation key matching the originating `Block::ToolCall.id`. Will become `ContentPart::ToolResult.tool_use_id` when re-compiled.
- `Block::ToolResult.is_error`: success/failure flag. Mirrors `ContentPart::ToolResult.is_error`. Default semantics: false on success, true when the registry returned a `ToolError` or the tool's call body returned `Err`.
- `Block::ToolResult.name` is RETAINED. ContentPart::ToolResult does not carry a name (Anthropic protocol doesn't need it because correlation is via id), but History benefits from it for human inspection / debugging. The Agent simply drops the name when compiling to ContentPart.

Cascade fix-ups -- the new field shape WILL force exhaustive `match` updates:
- src/agent.rs production match (around line 155-170) that already destructures `Block::ReasoningTrace { .. } | Block::ToolCall { .. } | Block::ToolResult { .. } => { ... }` continues to use `{ .. }` so this compiles unchanged. Verify by build.
- src/messagebus.rs route() (around line 152-168) destructures `Block::AgentMessage { .. }` only -- no impact.
- src/history.rs tests do NOT currently construct ToolCall/ToolResult variants -- no impact expected.
- src/test_support.rs and tests/anthropic_live.rs: scan for any uses of Block::ToolCall/ToolResult; none expected, but verify.
- If clippy/rustc flags any other exhaustive-match site, update with minimum changes (zero logic changes).

Tests to add (inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in src/history.rs):
- A test that appends a Block::ToolCall to a History and confirms via pattern-match that all four fields (id, name, arguments, timestamp) round-trip via linearize().
- A test that appends a Block::ToolResult (with `is_error: true`) and confirms all five fields round-trip via linearize().
- A test that appends a paired (ToolCall, ToolResult) sequence sharing the same id/tool_use_id and confirms the correlation key survives linearize().

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO:
- Touch src/agent.rs production logic beyond what is mechanically required for compilation. The existing skip arm for tool blocks remains in place.
- Wire Block::ToolCall/Block::ToolResult into Agent's History-to-ContentPart compilation (separate bead).
- Touch src/model.rs, src/tool.rs, src/harness.rs, src/messagebus.rs, src/stdio.rs, src/rpc.rs (except minimum no-op match arms forced by exhaustiveness checks).
- Update DESIGN.md, ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Add dependencies.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md: no banner comments, no decorative dividers, no usage examples in doc-comments.
- Named-field (struct-style) variants.

Definition of Done:
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (zero warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` (within 120s)

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl or .beads/last-touched. Use the trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Process: Forbid premature 'in_progress' bead state mutation before delegation
description:
PROBLEM (observed on bd-ike / Phase A3):
The Coordinator ran `br update bd-ike --status in_progress` BEFORE delegating
the bead. This left `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/last-touched` dirty in
the working copy when the Coding Subagent started. The subagent's content
commit then briefly absorbed those bead-state changes, which had to be
stripped out by the close recipe. For subsequent beads (bd-119, bd-xub,
bd-2et) the Coordinator quietly skipped the in-progress mark and there was
no contamination.

WHY THIS IS PROCESS-IMPROVING:
The whole point of the two-commit close recipe (content commit + separate
bead-state commit) is to keep content commits free of bead-state churn so
they are atomic and revertable. Premature `br update --status in_progress`
defeats that invariant by leaking jsonl mutations into the content commit.
The current `coordinator.md` close recipe documents the closing transition
carefully but is silent on the opening transition. That silence is what
permitted the A3 leak.

PROPOSED CHANGE (touches `.claude/agents/coordinator.md`):
Add an explicit rule near the close recipe (or in a new "Bead state
discipline" subsection) that says:
  - Do NOT mark a bead `in_progress` via `br update --status in_progress`
    before delegating. Beads are implicitly in progress while a Coding
    Subagent is running; the only mutations the Coordinator performs are
    `br close` (after Judge passes) and `br update --status done` if used.
  - The working copy MUST be clean of `.beads/*` modifications before
    delegating to any subagent.
  - If `.beads/*` files are dirty, run the bead-state commit FIRST, then
    delegate.

ACCEPTANCE:
- coordinator.md gains an explicit "do not mark in_progress" rule.
- coordinator.md gains a "working copy must be clean before delegation" rule.
- Single atomic commit on a fresh `@`, with trailer.

title: Define JSON-RPC server trait and implementation
description: Create src/rpc.rs with a #[rpc(server)] trait defining three methods: initialize (returns agent_id + owner_id), send_message (agent_id + content, returns ok:true, implicitly subscribes to agent events), shutdown (triggers graceful shutdown). The trait impl holds Arc to shared Harness state (bus, agent_id, owner_id, shutdown CancellationToken). send_message calls bus.send_inbound and spawns a task that subscribes to agent events and forwards them as JSON-RPC notifications via jsonrpsee subscription sink. Add pub mod rpc to src/lib.rs.

title: Phase D3: Top-level pristine_config::load orchestration
description:
Plan: blueprint/configuration-file/plan-configuration-file.md (Phase D3).

Add the public entry point: pub fn load(args: LoadArgs) -> Result<Config, ConfigErrors>. LoadArgs carries the CLI inputs:
- config: Option<PathBuf>  -- replaces embedded default.toml entirely
- auth: Option<PathBuf>    -- overrides default ~/pristine-auth.toml path

load() orchestrates the full pipeline:
1. Resolve topology path (D1). If None, use embedded default.toml (include_str! placeholder for now; Phase E1 will ship the real asset).
2. Resolve auth path (D1). If missing, auto-write template (D2).
3. Read both files to strings.
4. Parse to toml::Value, run templating (B3), then deserialize to TopologyConfig + AuthConfig (B2).
5. Validate tool refs (C2) and resolve aliases (C1).
6. Collect errors at each stage (C3); return Err(ConfigErrors) if any.
7. Return Ok(Config).

Constraints:
- Embedded default.toml is a placeholder for this bead (e.g. include_str!("../default.toml") with a TODO if needed). Phase E1 lands the real asset.
- load() never panics, never reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY directly — that lives in templating.
- Integration-style test in src/config (or tests/) that drives load() end-to-end with a fixture topology + fixture auth referencing {{TEST_VAR}} and asserts the returned Config matches expectations.

title: Hoist MapEnv test fixture into a shared config test_support module
description:
## Problem

The `MapEnv` test fixture — an in-memory `EnvSource` impl used to feed deterministic env vars to templating / parse / assemble tests — is duplicated in three test modules with near-identical bodies:

- `src/config/template.rs:165` (`struct MapEnv(HashMap<String, String>)`)
- `src/config/parse.rs:267` (same shape)
- `src/config.rs:129` (same shape, only differs in spelling `StdHashMap` for the import alias)

Each copy is roughly:

```rust
#[derive(Default)]
struct MapEnv(HashMap<String, String>);

impl MapEnv {
    fn new<const N: usize>(entries: [(&str, &str); N]) -> Self { ... }
}

impl EnvSource for MapEnv {
    fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<String> {
        self.0.get(name).cloned()
    }
}
```

Phases D1/D2/D3 and E1/E2 are queued and will each add new `#[cfg(test)]` blocks that exercise the env-source pathway (auto-write template, discovery rules, top-level `load`). Without a shared helper, each new bead is likely to add a fourth, fifth copy — the exact 'test-helper proliferation' pattern the tidy role guards against.

The crate already has `src/test_support.rs` (a `pub(crate)` module of cross-cutting test fixtures). That module is the natural home for this helper.

## Scope (single commit)

1. In `src/test_support.rs`, add a `pub(crate)` `MapEnv` struct gated on `#[cfg(test)]` (or alternatively in a new `pub(crate) mod config_test_support;` if the implementor prefers per-domain segregation; either is acceptable). It implements `crate::config::EnvSource` and has the same `new<const N: usize>(entries: [(&str, &str); N]) -> Self` constructor surface as today.
2. Delete the three duplicate definitions from `src/config/template.rs`, `src/config/parse.rs`, and `src/config.rs`.
3. Update each test module's `use` lines to import the shared `MapEnv`.

## Out of scope

- Changing any test assertions or body logic.
- Renaming `MapEnv` or its constructor.
- Hoisting any other test fixtures (the existing `StubArModel`, `EchoTool`, `StubShell` already live in `test_support.rs`; leave them).

## Definition of done

- `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- `cargo clippy` passes with no warnings (no `dead_code` warnings for unused fixtures).
- `cargo nextest run` passes; every config test that previously used a local `MapEnv` now uses the shared one.
- Exactly one definition of `MapEnv` exists in the codebase.

title: Unify agent.rs test drain idioms behind drain_until_idle helper
description:
Tidy/Reflection finding from end of Phase 2. The agent.rs test module has accreted three distinct drain idioms over the phase:

2. Inline 'idles == N' counter in 'agent_run_compiles_block_tool_call_into_content_part_tool_use' (line ~726) and 'agent_run_compiles_block_tool_result_into_content_part_tool_result' (line ~799).
3. Shared 'drain_until_idle' helper used in the three bd-1um tests (line ~843, called at ~926, ~1041, ~1135).

The helper exists but is only applied uniformly to the bd-1um tests. The earlier patterns were each added when a new bead required them and were never folded back. As Phase 3 adds new agent test patterns, this will compound.

## Scope

1. Generalize the helper into 'drain_until_idles(outbound, sink, n)' that accumulates events until the Nth Idle is observed. Document with a one-line doc-comment.
2. Replace all three idiom variants in src/agent.rs tests with calls to the unified helper.
3. Preserve every test's existing assertion semantics. The events vector each test inspects MUST contain the same events as before (events before the terminating Idle).
4. Do NOT change any non-test code in src/agent.rs.

## Acceptance

- Only one drain idiom remains in src/agent.rs tests.
- cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features, cargo nextest run all pass.
- Test count unchanged.
- No new dependencies.

## Context

Filed by the Reflection Agent at end of Phase 2 (Tool Calls). The prior Tidy passes (3 of them) examined documentation drift and fixture duplication but did not look at test-helper proliferation. This bead closes that gap before Phase 3 (Configuration File) adds new test patterns.

title: Phase 2 Step 5a: Add ToolSpec type and tools field to ModelInput
description:
Introduce a ToolSpec type in src/model.rs and extend ModelInput with a `tools: Vec<ToolSpec>` field. This is the metadata channel by which the Agent communicates the available tools to the Model per call. The Anthropic adapter will serialize this into the request body in the next bead.

Architectural rationale:
- ModelInput is the stable contract between Agent (or ContextCompiler) and Model. Provider request schemas (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini) bundle tool definitions alongside messages -- representing tools as a field of ModelInput keeps the trait surface uniform across providers without polluting Turn/ContentPart.
- ToolSpec is a small, provider-agnostic descriptor (`name`, `description`, `input_schema`). It is NOT `dyn Tool` -- it carries no behavior, only the JSON-schemaable shape needed to advertise the tool to the model. Adapters use ToolSpec to build provider-native request payloads; the Agent's ToolRegistry remains the source of truth for invocation.

Scope -- src/model.rs only (with cascade fix-ups, none expected):

1. Add `pub struct ToolSpec` with:
   ```rust
   pub struct ToolSpec {
       pub name: String,
       pub description: String,
       pub input_schema: serde_json::Value,
   }
   ```
   Derive `Clone, Debug`. No `Serialize`/`Deserialize` -- providers will translate ToolSpec into their own payload types.

2. Add `pub tools: Vec<ToolSpec>` field to the existing `pub struct ModelInput`.
   - Default to empty when the Agent has no tools (callers populate as needed).
   - Update the existing in-file constructor literal in the `mod tests` block (`ModelInput { turns: vec![...] }`) to also set `tools: Vec::new()` where required. Same for any literal usages elsewhere.

Cascade fix-ups -- forced by `tools` field addition:
- src/agent.rs Agent::run constructs `ModelInput { turns }` (around line 174). Update to `ModelInput { turns, tools: Vec::new() }` since the registry is not yet wired (separate bead). Single mechanical change.
- src/model/anthropic.rs serializes ModelInput. The current `model_input_to_anthropic` does not name `tools`. Build should still compile because the function only reads `input.turns`, but the empty `tools` field is implicitly threaded through. Confirm by build.
- src/test_support.rs StubArModel::last_input captures the input -- field-level destructuring should still work because `ModelInput` is `Clone`.
- Tests in src/model.rs, src/agent.rs, src/model/anthropic.rs, src/test_support.rs, tests/anthropic_live.rs may construct `ModelInput { turns: ... }` literally; add `tools: Vec::new()`.

Tests (inside src/model.rs `mod tests`):
- Construct a `ToolSpec` literal with all three fields populated; pattern-match to confirm field access.
- Construct a `ModelInput` with a non-empty `tools` vector (one ToolSpec); assert `input.tools.len() == 1` and the inner fields round-trip.
- Confirm `ToolSpec: Send + Sync` via the existing `assert_send_sync` helper.

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO:
- Wire the Agent to populate `tools` from a ToolRegistry (separate bead).
- Wire the Anthropic adapter to serialize `tools` into the request (separate bead bd-5b).
- Add provider-native types (e.g. AnthropicTool) in src/model/anthropic.rs (separate bead bd-5b).
- Touch src/history.rs, src/tool.rs, src/harness.rs, src/messagebus.rs, src/stdio.rs, src/rpc.rs.
- Update DESIGN.md, ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Add dependencies.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md: no banner comments, no decorative dividers, no usage examples in doc-comments.
- ToolSpec field `input_schema` must NOT be `&serde_json::Value` -- ToolSpec owns its data so it can be cloned freely. (The Tool trait returns `&serde_json::Value`; the Agent or adapter clones into a ToolSpec when assembling the request.)

Definition of Done:
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (zero warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` (within 120s)

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl or .beads/last-touched. Use the trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Tidy: extract resolve_path helper before Read/Write/Insert land
description:
## Context

`src/builtins/edit.rs` defines a private `resolve_path(input: &str) -> Result<PathBuf, ToolError>` helper that:

1. Rejects empty paths (returns `InvalidPath { reason: "path is empty" }`).
2. Returns the path unchanged if absolute.
3. Otherwise joins the input against `std::env::current_dir()`, mapping any cwd error to `InvalidPath { reason: "cwd: ..." }`.

Per `blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md` (Decisions, "Path resolution"), Read, Write, and Insert will all accept "absolute paths and paths relative to the process cwd" -- the same resolution logic. Today this lives behind `EditError::InvalidPath`, which is per-tool dialect; the resolution policy itself is portable shape.

## Goal

Extract the path-resolution logic to a shared crate-private helper BEFORE Read, Write, and Insert land, so the three new tools immediately consume the shared helper instead of duplicating it 3 more times. Per ARCHITECTURE.md's "Portable shape, adapter dialect" principle, the resolution policy is shared across consumers and is therefore promotion-worthy now (1 current + 3 imminent = 4 consumers).

## Required changes

1. Add a new private module (e.g. `src/builtins/path.rs`) exposing:

   ```rust
   pub(crate) enum PathResolveError {
       Empty,
       Cwd(String),
   }

   pub(crate) fn resolve_path(input: &str) -> Result<PathBuf, PathResolveError>;
   ```

   The function returns a typed dialect-free error. The caller maps `PathResolveError` onto its own tool's `InvalidPath { reason }` variant. This preserves per-tool error ownership.

2. Update `src/builtins/edit.rs` to call the shared helper and map `PathResolveError::{Empty,Cwd}` onto `EditError::InvalidPath { reason }`.

3. Register the new module in `src/builtins.rs` (`mod path;`). Do not re-export it.

4. Existing edit unit tests continue to pass unchanged; the externally-observable error JSON (`{"kind":"invalid_path","reason":...}`) does not change.

## Out of scope

- Promoting the helper to a public API.
- Changing `EditError` variants or wire format.
- Read/Write/Insert implementation -- those land in their own beads and pick up the helper for free.

## Definition of done

- `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- `cargo clippy` passes with no warnings.
- `cargo nextest run` passes (114 tests + 6 skipped, same as today).
- `src/builtins/edit.rs` no longer defines `resolve_path`; it imports the shared helper.

title: Phase 2 Step 2: Extend ContentPart with ToolUse and ToolResult
description:
Extend the ModelInput contract so that tool use can be represented at the Model boundary. The ContentPart enum in src/model.rs is already marked `#[non_exhaustive]` precisely to accommodate this addition (see the comment above the enum). No Agent/Harness/Anthropic-adapter integration yet -- that lands in later beads. This bead is pure type-level extension plus tests.

Scope:
- In src/model.rs, extend `pub enum ContentPart` with two new variants:
  - `ToolUse { id: String, name: String, input: serde_json::Value }`
    - `id`: provider-issued opaque identifier for the tool-use request (used to correlate with its result).
    - `name`: tool name as registered in the ToolRegistry.
    - `input`: arguments passed to the tool (JSON value matching the tool's input_schema).
  - `ToolResult { tool_use_id: String, content: serde_json::Value, is_error: bool }`
    - `tool_use_id`: the `id` of the originating `ToolUse` part.
    - `content`: the JSON payload returned by the tool (or the error description if `is_error` is true).
    - `is_error`: true when the tool failed; false on success. Mirrors Anthropic's tool_result block shape and is the simplest representation that survives across providers.

- Update the existing comment above `ContentPart` to no longer say "future phases will add `ToolUse` and `ToolResult` variants" -- this bead implements that. Replace the comment with a brief, present-tense note explaining that the enum carries text and tool exchanges; keep `#[non_exhaustive]` because more variants (image, audio, etc.) are still anticipated.

- Add tests inside the existing `mod tests` block in src/model.rs:
  - Construct a `ModelInput` containing a `Turn` whose content holds a `ContentPart::ToolUse` and verify field access by pattern-match (basic smoke check).
  - Same for `ContentPart::ToolResult`.
  - Verify `ContentPart::ToolUse` and `ContentPart::ToolResult` are `Send + Sync` (use the existing `assert_send_sync` helper).
  - Confirm `Clone` on `ContentPart::ToolResult` round-trips fields (use `is_error: true` and a JSON null content to keep it minimal).

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO any of the following in this bead:
- Touch src/agent.rs (do NOT change the History-to-ModelInput compilation; the existing arms that skip ToolCall/ToolResult Blocks remain in place. A later bead will replace that skip).
- Touch src/model/anthropic.rs (do NOT serialize the new variants; the existing `model_input_to_anthropic` will simply ignore them via the non-exhaustive match wildcard if needed -- but adding a new wildcard arm that explicitly ignores ToolUse/ToolResult is a minimum-change extension and is acceptable. If the compiler does NOT force a wildcard because the function only matches role-level structure, do not add one.)
- Touch src/history.rs (Block::ToolCall does not gain an id field in this bead -- that is a separate bead).
- Add new ModelStreamEvent variants -- that is a separate bead.
- Touch DESIGN.md or ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Add dependencies.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies in Cargo.toml.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` in non-test code.
- RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md: no banner comments, no decorative dividers, no usage examples in doc-comments, comments concise and professional.
- The two new variants must use struct-style (named) fields for forward compatibility, matching the style of Block::ToolCall and Block::ToolResult in src/history.rs.

Definition of Done (verify before reporting success):
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (no warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` (within 120s)

If the Anthropic adapter's match on ContentPart now produces a non-exhaustive-match warning because new variants exist, add a minimal explicit arm in `model_input_to_anthropic` that maps ToolUse/ToolResult to a no-op (push nothing to the messages text accumulator). Document with a single-line comment that the real handling lands in a later bead. This is an EXCEPTION to the "out of scope" rule for src/model/anthropic.rs and is permitted only if required to silence the warning.

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl -- the Coordinator handles bead-state commits separately. Use the trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Clean up emacs auto-save files and tighten .gitignore
description:

title: Phase 1 Step 7: Agent event loop
Reflection: capture Coordinator's bead-state-split recipe as a documented script
description:
## Problem

Each closed bead currently costs the Coordinator a ~10-step mechanical recipe split across two commits: bead-state checkpoint, then the actual content commit, with `br close`, `cp`, `jj restore`, `jj new`, `cp`, `jj desc` interleaved. Across ~25 beads in the Built-in Tools inter-phase this is roughly 250 mechanical steps consuming Coordinator context.

The mechanics are correct — they keep `.beads/issues.jsonl` state changes in their own atomic commit, separate from the content. But the recipe is undocumented (it lives only in the Coordinator's running context) and re-derived per bead.

## Action

Add a section to `.claude/agents/coordinator.md` named something like "## Bead close recipe" that:

1. Documents the canonical recipe step-by-step (the exact sequence of `br close`, `cp`, `jj restore -r @-`, `jj new`, `cp`, `jj desc`, `jj new` calls — or whatever the current sequence is).
2. Names the two commits the recipe produces: `Close <id>: <title>` (content) and `Close <id> bead state` (jsonl-only).
3. Notes the rationale: `.beads/beads.db` is gitignored so only `.beads/issues.jsonl` and `.beads/last-touched` need to be in the bead-state commit.

The recipe does NOT need to be a script (the user has not asked for scripting). Documenting it in the agent doc is enough — it stops re-derivation and lets the next Coordinator copy-paste.

## Acceptance

- `.claude/agents/coordinator.md` has a clear, copy-pasteable recipe.
- Single commit, standard trailer.
- No code changes.

title: Built-in tools P-1: ToolError::Execution(serde_json::Value) evolution
description:
## Context

Prelude bead 1/4 of the Built-in Tools inter-phase (plan at blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md, refined prompt at blueprint/builtin-tools/PROMPT.md). Lays the structural foundation: every built-in tool will surface errors as JSON Values, not strings, so per-tool typed error enums can serialize through one portable carrier (the "Portable shape, adapter dialect" principle in ARCHITECTURE.md).

## Required reading before coding

1. blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md (sections "Decisions", "Type definitions", "ToolError::Execution(serde_json::Value) evolution", "P-1")
2. blueprint/builtin-tools/PROMPT.md (the "Error model" bullet)
3. ARCHITECTURE.md (the "Portable shape, adapter dialect" principle at the top)
4. src/tool.rs (current ToolError definition + Display impl)
5. src/builtins.rs (current AddTool::call implementation; uses ToolError::Execution(String))
6. src/lib.rs (search for "ToolError::Execution" and the Agent::run tool-dispatch arm)
7. src/agent.rs (the tool-call dispatch loop -- look for ToolError handling)

## Goal

Change `ToolError::Execution(String)` to `ToolError::Execution(serde_json::Value)`. All call sites compile. All existing tests continue to pass.

## Required changes

1. In src/tool.rs:
   - Change the `Execution` variant to carry `serde_json::Value`.
   - Update the `Display` impl so the Execution arm renders the Value via `serde_json::to_string(...)` (fall back to a literal `{}` JSON if to_string fails -- it cannot fail for a well-formed Value, but be defensive without unwrap).

2. In src/agent.rs (or wherever the tool-dispatch loop wraps a ToolError into a Value for the ToolResult content):
   - In the `Err(err)` arm of the tool-call dispatch, forward the Value verbatim when the variant is `ToolError::Execution(v)`. For other variants, wrap as `serde_json::json!({"error": <other.to_string()>})`. Pattern:
     ```rust
     let value = match err {
         ToolError::Execution(v) => v,
         other => serde_json::json!({"error": other.to_string()}),
     };
     ```

3. In src/builtins.rs (AddTool::call):
   - Where it currently returns `Err(ToolError::Execution("...".into()))`, replace the string with `serde_json::json!({"error": "<same message>"})`. This is the minimal cascade; the typed `AddError` enum lands in P-2, not here.

4. Any other call site that constructs `ToolError::Execution(...)` must be updated similarly. Use `rg "ToolError::Execution"` to find them.

## Out of scope

- Introducing `AddError` -- that is P-2.
- Touching any other tool or creating new submodules -- those are later beads.
- Changing the public signature of `Tool::call` (it already returns `Result<Value, ToolError>` -- only the contents of the Execution variant change).

## Definition of Done

- `cargo fmt --check` passes
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` passes (no warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` passes within 120s
- The diff is small and additive in style; no drive-by refactors or formatting sweeps
- One atomic commit with the project's standard trailer

## Plan-respect note

This bead is foundational for "Portable shape, adapter dialect": the Value carrier is the portable shape; per-tool typed enums (added in later beads) are the adapter dialect.

title: Add 'Design principles' section to ARCHITECTURE.md (Portable shape, adapter dialect)
description:
Inter-phase doc bead. Capture the foundational design principle Pristine has been walking since Phase 1: a portable layer that carries provider-neutral information, and adapters at the periphery that own provider-specific dialect.

This principle is referenced in the upcoming inter-phase work (Read/Write/Edit/Insert/ExecBash) -- making it explicit before the next coding subagent reads ARCHITECTURE.md.

Scope -- ARCHITECTURE.md only. No source code changes.

Insert a new top-level section titled `## Design Principles` between the document's opening text and the existing `## Goals` section. Section body:

```markdown
## Design Principles

### Portable shape, adapter dialect

Pristine's portable layer -- the engine, traits, and types that agents
and clients touch -- carries only the information that every targeted
provider, model, or client requires. Provider dialect (wire formats,
naming conventions, optional fields, error vocabularies, advertisement
mechanisms) lives in adapters at the periphery. The portable layer
never grows speculatively; a capability is promoted from
"adapter-specific" into the portable layer only when it surfaces as a
common pattern across multiple providers.

The principle has four consequences:

1. **Information vs. dialect.** Portable types capture *what* a turn,
   tool, or stream event is. Adapters capture *how* one provider
   encodes it on the wire. The split is load-bearing.
2. **No leaks downward.** When a feature is unique to one provider,
   the rest of the engine never learns about it. Substituting providers
   does not propagate changes through the core.
3. **Additive evolution.** The portable layer grows when a pattern is
   shared, not when one provider gains a feature. Promotion happens
   with at least two cross-provider examples in view -- never on
   speculation about one.
4. **Separation of concerns at every boundary.** System prompts carry
   identity and behavior. Tools carry behavior and self-description.
   Adapters carry transport and dialect. Clients carry presentation.
   Each layer owns what it owns and nothing else.

The Goals listed below -- composability, multi-agent isolation,
transport neutrality -- are consequences of this principle, not peer
principles.
```

Constraints:
- Doc-only. NO source code changes. NO Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock changes. NO changes to any file other than ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Section goes ABOVE `## Goals`, AFTER the title.
- Line wrap consistent with the rest of ARCHITECTURE.md (paragraphs at ~80 columns or whatever the surrounding text uses -- match existing).
- No trailing whitespace.
- Heading levels: `## Design Principles` is a top-level section; the principle name is `### Portable shape, adapter dialect`. No deeper headers.

Definition of Done:
- `git diff --check` clean (no whitespace errors).
- `cargo nextest run` passes (sanity check; no source changes, but confirm).
- The new section renders coherently: a reader landing on ARCHITECTURE.md sees the principle BEFORE the Goals section.

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl or .beads/last-touched. Use trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Edit T-3: unit tests covering every typed error + edge case
description:
## Context

Third Edit slice. Adds unit-test coverage for `Edit::call` exercising every typed error variant + every documented edge case.

## Required reading

1. blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md (sections "Edit" under behavioral spec; "Testing strategy")
2. blueprint/builtin-tools/PROMPT.md (the "Edit" bullet)
3. src/builtins/edit.rs (the impl just landed in bd-ctk)
4. src/builtins/exec_bash.rs (template for `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block, especially for tests that need a temp working dir / filesystem fixture)
5. RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md (test code may use unwrap/expect freely)

## Goal

Add a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block to `src/builtins/edit.rs` with comprehensive coverage. Test count should increase by at least the 9 distinct scenarios listed below. No changes to production code.

## Required test scenarios

Each is one `#[tokio::test]` (use the same `#[tokio::test]` style that ExecBash tests use):

1. **`edit_replaces_single_match_writes_file_atomically`**: Fixture has content `"foo bar baz"`. Call Edit with `{path, old_str: "bar", new_str: "BAR"}`. Assert:
   - `Ok(value)` with `value["replaced"] == true`.
   - File on disk now reads `"foo BAR baz"`.

2. **`edit_returns_multiple_matches_on_count_ge_2`**: Fixture has content `"foo foo foo"`. Call Edit with `old_str: "foo", new_str: "FOO"`. Assert:
   - `Err(ToolError::Execution(value))`.
   - `value["kind"] == "multiple_matches"`.
   - `value["count"] == 3`.
   - File on disk is UNCHANGED.

3. **`edit_returns_no_matches_on_zero`**: Fixture has content `"hello"`. Call Edit with `old_str: "xyz", new_str: "..."`. Assert:
   - `value["kind"] == "no_matches"`.
   - File unchanged.

4. **`edit_returns_no_matches_on_empty_old_str`**: Fixture has content `"hello"`. Call Edit with `old_str: "", new_str: "x"`. Assert:
   - `value["kind"] == "no_matches"` (per the documented decision).
   - File unchanged.

5. **`edit_returns_file_not_found_on_missing_path`**: Use a path that definitely does not exist (e.g., `tmpdir/nonexistent-{uuid}.txt`). Assert:
   - `value["kind"] == "file_not_found"`.
   - `value["path"]` matches the resolved path.

6. **`edit_returns_not_utf8_for_binary_file`**: Write a fixture file containing bytes `[0x48, 0x69, 0xFF, 0x80]` (Hi + invalid UTF-8). Call Edit with `old_str: "anything", new_str: "x"`. Assert:
   - `value["kind"] == "not_utf8"`.
   - `value["byte_offset"] == 2` (the position of the first invalid byte).

7. **`edit_returns_invalid_path_on_empty_string`**: Call Edit with `path: ""`. Assert:
   - `value["kind"] == "invalid_path"`.
   - `value["reason"]` is a non-empty string.

8. **`edit_old_eq_new_is_noop_success`**: Fixture has content `"hello"`. Call Edit with `old_str: "hello", new_str: "hello"`. Assert:
   - `Ok` with `value["replaced"] == true`.
   - File on disk unchanged (mtime may differ but content is identical -- you only need to assert content).

9. **`edit_preserves_trailing_newline`**: Fixture has content `"hello\n"`. Call Edit with `old_str: "hello", new_str: "HELLO"`. Assert:
   - `Ok({replaced: true})`.
   - File on disk now reads `"HELLO\n"` (newline preserved).

10. (Optional) **`edit_returns_invalid_input_on_malformed_json`**: Call `tool.call(json!({"path": "x"}))` (missing old_str/new_str). Assert `Err(ToolError::InvalidInput(_))`.

## Test fixtures

Use a per-test unique directory under `std::env::temp_dir()`. Generate uniqueness via `Uuid::new_v4().simple().to_string()` (uuid crate is in deps; same pattern ExecBash uses). Create the dir at the start of each test; do not bother cleaning up at the end (the OS reclaims temp dirs eventually).

Example helper inside the tests module:

```rust
fn unique_tempdir() -> std::path::PathBuf {
    use uuid::Uuid;
    let id = Uuid::new_v4().simple().to_string();
    let dir = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("pristine-edit-test-{id}"));
    std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).expect("create temp dir");
    dir
}
```

Use `std::fs::write` synchronously for fixture setup. The tool itself uses `tokio::fs`, so tests run as `#[tokio::test]`.

Tests freely use `unwrap()`/`expect()` (RUST_STYLE_GUIDE allows in test code).

## Out of scope

- Registration (T-4).
- ARCHITECTURE.md updates (T-4).
- Tests for OTHER tools (each tool gets its own T-3).
- Integration / smoke tests (no live Edit smoke test required by the plan — Edit doesn't spawn anything).

## Definition of Done

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` -- test count INCREASES by at least 9 from the baseline of 104 passed, 6 skipped. New count: 113+ passed, 6 skipped.
- `rg "\.unwrap\(\)|\.expect\(" src/builtins/edit.rs` may now return hits — but every hit must be inside the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block.
- `jj diff --summary` shows exactly `M src/builtins/edit.rs`. No other files.
- Commit subject `Close bd-XXX: Edit T-3 (tests)` with the trailer.

## Plan-respect note

Tests verify the dialect (per-tool typed errors) without coupling to anything portable. Each variant of `EditError` gets its own assertion target — the dialect is fully exercised.

title: Write: types + impl + tests + registration (full slice)
description:
## Context

Compressed Write bead. Pattern follows Edit and Read; uses shared `resolve_path` and `atomic_write` from `src/builtins/path.rs`.

## Required reading

1. PLAN.md (sections "Write" under behavioral spec; "Per-tool error enums" -> WriteError; "Per-tool input shapes")
2. PROMPT.md (the "Write" bullet)
3. src/builtins/read.rs (most recent template — clean tool shape)
4. src/builtins/edit.rs (uses `atomic_write` — the same call Write needs)
5. src/builtins/path.rs (shared helpers)
6. RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md

## Required deliverables

### 1. NEW `src/builtins/write.rs`

```rust
use crate::builtins::path::{atomic_write, resolve_path, AtomicWriteError, PathResolveError};
use crate::tool::ToolError;

#[derive(serde::Deserialize)]
struct WriteInput {
    path: String,
    content: String,
}

#[derive(serde::Serialize)]
#[serde(tag = "kind", rename_all = "snake_case")]
enum WriteError {
    InvalidPath { reason: String },
    PermissionDenied { path: String },
    IoError { reason: String },
}

fn err(e: WriteError) -> ToolError { /* like other tools, uses unwrap_or_else */ }

#[derive(serde::Serialize)]
struct WriteOutput {
    bytes_written: u64,
}

pub struct Write;

impl Write {
    pub fn new() -> Self { Write }
}

impl Default for Write { fn default() -> Self { Self::new() } }

// Tool impl using the same async-trait pattern as Edit/Read.
```

### 2. Tool::call behavior

1. Resolve `path` via `resolve_path`. Map `PathResolveError` → `WriteError::InvalidPath`.
2. Compute parent dir via `resolved.parent()`. If parent is Some(non-empty), `tokio::fs::create_dir_all(parent)`. On error:
   - If `e.kind() == ErrorKind::PermissionDenied`, map to `WriteError::PermissionDenied { path: resolved.display().to_string() }`.
   - Else map to `WriteError::IoError { reason: format!("create_dir_all: {e}") }`.
3. Call `atomic_write(&resolved, parsed.content.as_bytes()).await`. Map `AtomicWriteError`:
   - For `WriteTmp(msg)` and `Rename(msg)`: if the message contains "Permission denied" (case insensitive — use `.to_ascii_lowercase().contains("permission denied")`), map to `PermissionDenied`. Otherwise map to `IoError { reason: format!("write tmp: {msg}") }` or `IoError { reason: format!("rename: {msg}") }` matching Edit's pattern.
4. Return `WriteOutput { bytes_written: parsed.content.len() as u64 }`.

### 3. Edge cases (PLAN)

- Existing file: silently overwritten (atomic-rename takes care of this).
- `path` is a directory: rename will fail with an `IoError`. (Don't pre-detect; let the rename fail.)
- `content` empty: file is created empty; `bytes_written = 0`.

### 4. Update `src/builtins.rs`

Add `pub mod write;` and `pub use write::Write;`.

### 5. Register in `src/lib.rs`

Add `.add_tool(Arc::new(Write::new()))`. Widen import:
```rust
use crate::builtins::{AddTool, Edit, ExecBash, Read, Write};
```

### 6. ARCHITECTURE.md

Replace the `### Write` stub:

```markdown
### Write

Atomic file write with auto-created parent directories. Input:
`{path, content}` (content is UTF-8; the `String` type guarantees it).
Output: `{bytes_written: u64}`. The target path is resolved (absolute
or cwd-relative); the parent directory is created recursively if it
does not exist. The write itself uses the shared atomic-rename helper:
content is written to `{path}.tmp` then renamed atop `{path}`,
guaranteeing readers see either the old file or the new file but
never a partial write. Existing files are silently overwritten. Error
variants: `InvalidPath { reason }`, `PermissionDenied { path }`,
`IoError { reason }`. Permission errors during parent-dir creation or
the atomic-rename steps surface as `PermissionDenied`.
```

### 7. Unit tests (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests`)

At least these 6 `#[tokio::test]`s:

1. `write_creates_file_with_content` — happy path. Write `"hello"` to a fresh path; assert `bytes_written == 5`; read file back via std::fs::read_to_string and assert content matches.
2. `write_overwrites_existing_file` — write fixture "old"; write new with content "new"; assert file now reads "new".
3. `write_auto_creates_parent_directory` — write to `{tempdir}/nested/dir/file.txt`; assert file exists, parent dir exists.
4. `write_empty_content_creates_empty_file` — write `""`; assert `bytes_written == 0`, file exists, is empty.
5. `write_invalid_path_empty_string` — call with `path: ""`; assert `kind == "invalid_path"`.
6. `write_handles_existing_directory_as_target` — pre-create a directory at the target path; call write; assert `Err` with `kind` either `io_error` or `permission_denied` (the IO layer's choice is acceptable). Document which one is observed.

Use `unique_tempdir()` like edit.rs and read.rs.

## Constraints

- No `unwrap()`/`expect()` in production.
- No new Cargo deps.
- Test count +6 (currently 125 → ≥131 passed, 6 skipped).
- Files: `A src/builtins/write.rs`, `M src/builtins.rs`, `M src/lib.rs`, `M ARCHITECTURE.md`.
- Commit has trailer.

## jj workflow

`jj desc -m "$(printf 'Close bd-XXX: Write tool (types + impl + tests + registration)\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>\n')"`

Verify with `jj log -r @ --no-graph -T 'description'`.

## Definition of Done

- fmt / clippy / nextest all clean.
- 131+ passed, 6 skipped.
- Diff is exactly 4 files.
- Trailer present.

## Plan-respect note

`atomic_write` is the cross-tool portable helper (now shared by Edit and Write); per-tool dialect (WriteError vs EditError) remains module-private. The permission-denied substring heuristic is the dialect responding to a Linux/Unix error message.

title: Tidy: ARCHITECTURE.md Built-in Tools section reads as if all five are implemented
description:
## Context

`ARCHITECTURE.md` line 389:

> The harness registers five built-in tools that give the agent direct filesystem and shell capabilities: Read, Write, Edit, Insert, and ExecBash.

But the file then lists three real subsections (Read, Write, Insert) as stubs that say "Stub -- filled in by the [...] registration bead." The opening sentence is in the present tense and implies all five are registered now, which is misleading: a reader of ARCHITECTURE.md today will incorrectly conclude that Read, Write, and Insert already exist.

The accurate state today (per `src/lib.rs` `add_tool` calls): three tools are registered -- AddTool, ExecBash, Edit. (Note also that AddTool is intentionally excluded from the "Built-in Tools" narrative; it is a demo and is covered in the Phase 2 section above.)

## Goal

Make the "Built-in Tools" opening paragraph in `ARCHITECTURE.md` match reality without diverging from the eventual end-state. The cleanest fix is one sentence that distinguishes "will register" from "currently registers":

> The harness will register five built-in tools: Read, Write, Edit, Insert, and ExecBash. As of this writing, Edit and ExecBash are implemented; Read, Write, and Insert have placeholder subsections below and will be filled in as their registration beads land.

(Wording is illustrative; the implementer should pick the cleanest phrasing.)

## Required changes

1. Edit the opening paragraph of the `## Built-in Tools` section in `ARCHITECTURE.md` (around line 389) to distinguish current state from end state. Keep the sentence short.

2. Do NOT modify the per-tool subsections; the stubs themselves are accurate.

## Out of scope

- Filling in the Read/Write/Insert stubs (their respective implementation beads handle that).
- Reorganizing the section.

## Definition of done

- The "Built-in Tools" opening no longer claims five tools are registered today.
- `git diff` is limited to a small wording change in ARCHITECTURE.md.

title: Coordinator: codify Tidy/Reflection bead-creation commit description format
description:
## Why

Per bd-38h, the Coordinator now commits Tidy / Reflection bead-creation as a descriptive commit before the next Coding Subagent starts. Two examples have landed:

- `Reflection: file bd-1y8, bd-38h, bd-30l for coordinator/coding process improvements`
- `Tidy: file bd-2nz/bd-1rx/bd-3dd/bd-msn for post-Phase-C entropy`

The two are cosmetically inconsistent: comma-space separators vs slashes, and the second omits a trailing punctuation period. The format is fine in either form, but a single canonical form is easier to grep and easier to enforce in future Reflection passes.

## Concrete observation

Searching `git log --oneline --grep='Tidy: file\|Reflection: file'` across the recent window returns commits that look syntactically different but mean the same thing. This is low-stakes but worth codifying once, since the messages will accumulate over the project lifetime and become a navigable index.

## What to change

In `.claude/agents/coordinator.md` under "Bead state discipline" section 3 (Bead-creation hygiene by Tidy / Reflection), pin the commit-description form:

> The commit description follows the canonical form:
> ```
> <Tidy|Reflection>: file bd-XXX, bd-YYY, bd-ZZZ for <one-line reason>
> ```
> Bead IDs are comma-space separated. The reason is a short noun phrase, not a sentence; no trailing period.

## Acceptance

- `.claude/agents/coordinator.md` Bead state discipline section 3 contains the canonical-form template.
- No other files changed.

title: ExecBash T-4: register in HarnessBuilder + fill ARCHITECTURE.md ExecBash stub
description:
## Context

Fourth and final ExecBash slice. Wires the `ExecBash` tool into the harness's default registration chain and replaces the ARCHITECTURE.md placeholder stub with the real per-tool paragraph.

After this bead, `pristine run` registers BOTH the existing `AddTool` AND the new `ExecBash` tool, so an agent invoked via the harness can actually exec shell commands.

## Required reading

1. blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md (sections "T-4. Registration"; "ExecBash" sub-section under "Per-tool behavioral specifications")
2. blueprint/builtin-tools/PROMPT.md (the "ExecBash" bullet)
3. src/lib.rs — find `run_async` (or wherever `HarnessBuilder` is constructed and tools are added). Look for the existing `add_tool(Arc::new(AddTool::new())?)` line — ExecBash will follow the same pattern.
4. ARCHITECTURE.md — locate the `### ExecBash` stub in the `## Built-in Tools` section (added by bd-76o, doc-prep).
5. src/builtins/exec_bash.rs — the actual ExecBash impl, to write an accurate paragraph.

## Goal

1. Register `ExecBash` in `HarnessBuilder` via `.add_tool(Arc::new(ExecBash::new())?)` (or `.add_tool(Arc::new(ExecBash::new()))` if `add_tool` is infallible — match the existing AddTool pattern).
2. Replace the ARCHITECTURE.md `### ExecBash` placeholder stub with a real paragraph documenting: input shape, output shape, error variants, and edge cases.

## Required changes

### 1. `src/lib.rs`

Find the existing line that registers AddTool (likely inside `run_async` or `default_harness_builder` — search for `AddTool::new` or `add_tool(Arc::new`). Add a corresponding line for `ExecBash::new()`. Use the same Arc/error-handling pattern as AddTool. The line should be alphabetically or logically adjacent (group built-ins together).

If `crate::builtins::ExecBash` is not yet importable at the registration site, add the import.

### 2. `ARCHITECTURE.md`

Replace the line `*Stub -- filled in by the ExecBash registration bead.*` under `### ExecBash` with a real paragraph. Suggested content (rephrase/restructure as you see fit; keep it concise and match the existing prose voice):

```markdown
### ExecBash

Executes a single bash command via the `Shell` trait (real impl: `BashShell`,
which spawns `/bin/bash -c <command>`). Input: `{command: String,
timeout_seconds?: u64}`; the default timeout is 30 seconds. Output:
`{stdout, stderr, status, stdout_truncated, stderr_truncated,
has_invalid_utf8_stdout, has_invalid_utf8_stderr, execution_id}`. The
returned stdout/stderr are the **last 64 KiB** of each stream
(tail-truncated), converted via `String::from_utf8_lossy`; the
`has_invalid_utf8_*` flags are `true` iff lossy conversion replaced any
bytes. `status` is one of `{status:"exit", code}`, `{status:"signal",
name}`, or `{status:"timeout"}`. `execution_id` is a hyphenless UUID v4
(32 hex chars) that names the full-output tmp files staged at
`tempdir()/pristine-{pid}/{execution_id}.{stdout,stderr}` (best-effort
write; failures are logged but do not fail the call). Error variants
(serialized as `ToolError::Execution(Value)`): `Spawn { reason }`, `Io {
reason }`, `TmpFile { reason }`. Malformed input (missing `command`,
non-string, etc.) surfaces as the engine-level `ToolError::InvalidInput`,
not an ExecBash-dialect error.
```

If the existing ExecBash stub has subtle nuance (e.g., specific wording or formatting), match it. Do NOT touch the other tool stubs.

## Out of scope

- Changes to other tools' stubs or registrations.
- Changes to SYSTEM_PROMPT (that's an EOC bead).
- Tests of any kind (those are in T-3, already done).
- DESIGN.md updates (EOC bead).

## Definition of Done

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` -- count unchanged at 104 passed, 6 skipped
- `jj diff --summary` shows exactly `M src/lib.rs` and `M ARCHITECTURE.md`. No other files.
- Commit subject `Close bd-XXX: ExecBash T-4 (registration)` (or similar) WITH the trailer.

## Plan-respect note

Registration is dialect-driven: each tool's registration call is a one-liner that the harness simply runs. The `add_tool` API is the portable interface; what each tool DOES is the dialect.

title: Edit T-4: register in HarnessBuilder + fill ARCHITECTURE.md Edit stub
description:
## Context

Fourth and final Edit slice. Registers `Edit` in `HarnessBuilder` and replaces the ARCHITECTURE.md `### Edit` placeholder with a real paragraph.

After this bead, `pristine run` exposes Edit alongside AddTool and ExecBash.

## Required reading

1. blueprint/builtin-tools/PLAN.md (T-4 Registration; Edit behavioral spec)
2. src/lib.rs — locate the existing `.add_tool(Arc::new(AddTool::new()))` and `.add_tool(Arc::new(ExecBash::new()))` chain. Edit follows the same pattern.
3. ARCHITECTURE.md — locate the `### Edit` stub line under `## Built-in Tools`.
4. src/builtins/edit.rs — the impl, to write an accurate paragraph.

## Required changes

### 1. `src/lib.rs`

Add `.add_tool(Arc::new(Edit::new()))` after ExecBash's registration. Use the same Arc / error-handling pattern. Widen the `use` import:
```rust
use crate::builtins::{AddTool, Edit, ExecBash};
```

### 2. `ARCHITECTURE.md`

Replace the `*Stub -- filled in by the Edit registration bead.*` line under `### Edit` with a real paragraph. Suggested content:

```markdown
### Edit

In-place text replacement with str_replace match-once safety. Input:
`{path, old_str, new_str}`. Output: `{replaced: bool}`. Behavior:
resolve the path (absolute or cwd-relative), read the file, validate
strict UTF-8, count exact-byte occurrences of `old_str`. If 0 → typed
`NoMatches` error; if ≥2 → typed `MultipleMatches { count }` error; if
exactly 1 → atomic-rename replace and return `{replaced: true}`. If
`old_str == new_str`, return `{replaced: true}` without writing
(documented no-op success). Empty `old_str` short-circuits to
`NoMatches`. Error variants (in `ToolError::Execution(Value)`):
`MultipleMatches { count }`, `NoMatches`, `FileNotFound { path }`,
`NotUtf8 { byte_offset }`, `InvalidPath { reason }`, `IoError {
reason }`. Malformed input surfaces as `ToolError::InvalidInput`. The
atomic-rename pattern (write `{path}.tmp`, then `rename` to `{path}`)
ensures Edit never leaves a partial write on disk.
```

Match the existing prose voice; do not modify other tool stubs.

## Out of scope

- Other tools' stubs.
- SYSTEM_PROMPT.
- Tests.
- DESIGN.md.

## Definition of Done

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` -- 114 passed, 6 skipped (unchanged from after bd-3qh)
- `jj diff --summary` shows exactly `M src/lib.rs` and `M ARCHITECTURE.md`.
- Commit subject `Close bd-XXX: Edit T-4 (registration)` with trailer.

## Plan-respect note

Registration is portable; Edit's behavior is dialect. The `add_tool` API doesn't care which tool you hand it; it's the stable plugin point.

description:
## Context

## Goal

Three additive changes to ARCHITECTURE.md. NO code changes.

## Required changes

1. **Append a paragraph to the existing `## Tool Calls` section** describing the construction surface for built-in tools:

   > Built-in tools follow a stable construction surface: each tool is a struct in `src/builtins/{name}.rs` with an explicit `Tool::new() -> Self` constructor. No behavioral arguments today; future hooks (audit, path policy, allowlists, metering) land additively on `new`. The bare `new()` is the stable plugin point.

   (Match the existing prose voice; rephrase as needed for flow. Place at the end of `## Tool Calls`.)

2. **Add a NEW `## Built-in Tools` section** AFTER `## Tool Calls`. Structure:

   ```markdown
   ## Built-in Tools

   The harness registers five built-in tools that give the agent direct
   filesystem and shell capabilities: Read, Write, Edit, Insert, and
   ExecBash. Each lives in its own non-mod-rs submodule under
   `src/builtins/`, with co-located tests. Each tool owns its own typed
   error enum (the dialect) and emits errors through the shared
   `ToolError::Execution(serde_json::Value)` carrier (the portable shape).

   ### Read

   *Stub -- filled in by the Read registration bead.*

   ### Write

   *Stub -- filled in by the Write registration bead.*

   ### Edit

   *Stub -- filled in by the Edit registration bead.*

   ### Insert

   *Stub -- filled in by the Insert registration bead.*

   ### ExecBash

   *Stub -- filled in by the ExecBash registration bead.*
   ```

   The stub text should be exactly `*Stub -- filled in by the <Tool> registration bead.*` (italic markdown placeholder). Per-tool registration beads will replace each stub with the real paragraph.

3. **Add an `ExecBash tmp-file storage` paragraph** as a subsection of `## Built-in Tools`, AFTER the per-tool subsections (the design decision has no other natural home in the codebase):

   ```markdown
   ### ExecBash tmp-file storage model

   ExecBash stages full stdout and stderr for each execution to per-process
   tmp files at `tempdir()/pristine-{pid}/{execution_id}.{stdout,stderr}`,
   with `execution_id` a UUID v4 (hyphenless). The 64 KiB tail returned
   in the tool's JSON response is the live capture buffer; the full
   stream lives on disk. The tmp directory is created lazily on first
   ExecBash invocation and removed at harness shutdown. This staging is
   forward-compat for a future Read-by-id tool that fetches full output
   by execution_id (out of scope this cycle).
   ```

## Out of scope

- ANY code changes. This bead is doc-only.
- Filling in the per-tool stubs -- those land in per-tool registration beads (T-4).

Documenting "portable shape, adapter dialect" in the Built-in Tools section keeps the principle visible at the spot where it shows up most concretely: every tool registered here owns a private typed error enum but emits through one portable carrier.

title: Fix silent hang when Agent dies with error
description:
## Goal
Two coupled fixes that both touch `src/lib.rs`. Land them together.

**Fix A — Silent hang on agent error.** The binary hangs silently when an Agent task dies (e.g., the Anthropic API returns 401 with a bad key). Subscribers cannot detect this because broadcast `Sender` clones in `Harness` and `InMemoryMessageBus` keep the channel open after the Agent task exits. Resolution: extend `AgentEvent` with an `Error { message: String }` variant; the Harness emits it from its per-agent spawn task when `agent.run()` returns `Err`; the binary's streaming loop treats `AgentEvent::Error` as terminal and returns `anyhow::Err`.

**Fix B — CLI surface cleanup.** The current binary accepts `1p run <message>`. Per `ARCHITECTURE.md` §"Initial Build" the two Owner messages are both hard-coded (`"Introduce yourself to me, Pristine"` and `"Write me a poem of what it is like to be you, Pristine"`). Remove the positional `message` arg from the `Run` subcommand. The `--model` flag stays.

## Reference
- `src/messagebus.rs` — `AgentEvent` enum
- `src/harness.rs` — `Harness::start` spawn body
- `src/lib.rs` — `run_async`, Clap parser, constants

## Required changes

### 1. `src/messagebus.rs`
Extend the enum:
```
pub enum AgentEvent {
    TokenDelta { text: String },
    BlockComplete { block: std::sync::Arc<crate::history::HistoryNode> },
    RunComplete { usage: crate::model::Usage },
    Error { message: String },
}
```
No other change. The `Error` variant carries a `Display`-formatted error message; the Harness constructs it directly. No `From` impls.

Add ONE test in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`:
- `subscribe_outbound_sees_published_error_events` — register an agent; subscribe; via the bus's outbound `Sender`, publish `AgentEvent::Error { message: "boom".into() }`; within `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), ...)`, assert the subscriber receives it and the message equals `"boom"`.

### 2. `src/harness.rs`
Modify ONLY the spawn body inside `Harness::start`. Pseudocode:
```rust
let outbound_for_emit = outbound.clone();
let cancel_for_task = self.cancel.clone();
let agent = AgentBuilder::new()
    .id(spec.id)
    .system_prompt(spec.system_prompt.clone())
    .model(ModelRole::Default, model.clone())
    .build(inbound, outbound)?;
let handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
    let res = tokio::select! {
        _ = cancel_for_task.cancelled() => Ok::<(), Error>(()),
        res = agent.run() => res.map_err(Error::from),
    };
    if let Err(ref e) = res {
        let _ = outbound_for_emit.send(AgentEvent::Error { message: e.to_string() });
        cancel_for_task.cancel();
    }
    res
});
```
Notes:
- The `cancel` clone may already exist in scope; reuse if so.
- The `let _ = ...` discards send failure (no subscribers = best-effort, per the broadcast-is-lossy convention).
- The post-emit `cancel.cancel()` ensures `run_until_shutdown` does not stall in multi-Agent futures.

Add ONE new test:
- `harness_emits_error_event_when_agent_dies` — build a Harness with one Agent whose `StubArModel` yields a single `Err(model::Error::Api { status: 500, message: "boom".into() })`. `start()`, subscribe, `send_to_agent` a `UserMessage`. Within `timeout(Duration::from_secs(2), ...)`, assert the subscriber receives an `AgentEvent::Error { message }` where `message.contains("500")` AND `message.contains("boom")` (the agent::Error's Display chain should include the model error's Display). Then `harness.shutdown()` (idempotent) and `harness.run_until_shutdown().await`; the latter must NOT hang.

A new test-local `StubArModel` will need to support yielding a non-empty error stream (the existing Step 6 stub returns `stream::empty()`). Either parameterize it or add a second helper in the same `#[cfg(test)]` module.

The existing four Step 6 tests should continue to pass without modification. If they break because of the new cancel-on-error behavior, adjust the failing test minimally; do NOT broadly refactor.

### 3. `src/lib.rs` — `run_async` streaming loop AND CLI surface

**CLI surface change (Fix B):**
The `Run` subcommand currently has fields `{ message: String, #[arg(long, default_value = "claude-sonnet-4-6")] model: String }`. **Remove the `message` field.** Final shape:
```rust
#[derive(Clap subcommand)]
enum Cmd {
    Run {
        #[arg(long, default_value = "claude-sonnet-4-6")]
        model: String,
    },
}
```
The first message is now a hard-coded constant alongside `SECOND_MESSAGE`:
```rust
const FIRST_MESSAGE: &str = "Introduce yourself to me, Pristine";
const SECOND_MESSAGE: &str = "Write me a poem of what it is like to be you, Pristine";
```
Inside `run_async`, after subscribing, call:
```rust
harness.send_to_agent(agent_id, owner_id, FIRST_MESSAGE.into())?;
```
Inside the `RunComplete` arm, after `runs_completed == 1`, send `SECOND_MESSAGE` instead of the old `args.message`-derived value.

**Streaming-loop change (Fix A):**
Add a new match arm BEFORE the `Ok(AgentEvent::BlockComplete { .. }) => {}` arm:
```rust
Ok(AgentEvent::Error { message }) => {
    return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("agent error: {message}"));
}
```

### 4. Style
- No banner comments.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- No `plan.md`/`bd-` references in source comments.

## Files you MAY modify
- `src/messagebus.rs`
- `src/harness.rs`
- `src/lib.rs`

## Files you may NOT touch
- `src/agent.rs`, `src/model.rs`, `src/model/anthropic.rs`, `src/history.rs`, `src/user.rs`
- `src/bin/pristine.rs`, `src/bin/1p.rs` (these one-line shims do not change)
- `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock` (no new deps; clap derive already covers the simplified subcommand)
- `.beads/`, documents, `tests/`

## Verification
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` — expect 27 tests pass (25 prior + 2 new: one in messagebus + one in harness) + 1 skipped
- `cargo build` — zero warnings
- `cargo run --bin 1p -- run --help` — Clap help should show ONLY the `--model` flag and NO positional `message` argument.
- Manual smoke test (Coordinator runs): `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=fakedeadbeef cargo run --bin 1p -- run` exits within ~10 seconds with a non-zero exit code and an `agent error: ...` message on stderr (do NOT hang).

## Out of scope
- Retry/recovery semantics
- Multi-Agent error handling beyond the cancel-on-error signal
- Changing broadcast channel capacity

title: Phase 1 Step 3: AR/DL Model traits
description:
## Goal
Define the model abstraction layer: `ARModel` trait (streaming completion), `DLModel` placeholder, `ModelStreamEvent`, `Usage`, `ModelRole`, and `model::Error`. No concrete implementation yet — Anthropic lands in Step 4.

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §Model (authoritative)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Step 3: AR/DL Model traits", §"`ModelStreamEvent`", §"`ModelRole` enum"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md`

## Implement in `src/model.rs`
- `pub enum ModelRole { Default }` — derive `Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash`. Plan.md commits to a single `Default` variant in Phase 1.
- `pub struct Usage { pub input_tokens: u32, pub output_tokens: u32 }` — derive `Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq`.
- `pub enum ModelStreamEvent` — variants exactly as in plan.md §"`ModelStreamEvent`":
  - `MessageStart { message_id: String, model: String }`
  - `ContentDelta { text: String }`
  - `ContentComplete { text: String }`
  - `Usage(Usage)` — keep this newtype-style variant so the `Usage` event from plan.md round-trips cleanly. (Plan.md lists both `MessageComplete { usage: Usage }` and `Usage { input_tokens, output_tokens }`; collapse the bare `Usage { … }` into `Usage(Usage)` and keep `MessageComplete { usage: Usage }` as a separate variant. Document the rationale in a single-line module comment.)
  - `MessageComplete { usage: Usage }`
  - `Error { message: String }`
  - Derive `Clone, Debug`.
- `pub enum Error` — typed model errors. Variants:
  - `Http(String)` (will carry `reqwest::Error`-derived messages in Step 4 without exposing reqwest types here — store `String` and convert via `From<reqwest::Error>` in `model/anthropic.rs`).
  - `Deserialization(String)` (likewise for SSE / JSON failures).
  - `Api { status: u16, message: String }` (HTTP non-2xx with a body).
  - `Configuration(String)` (missing API key, malformed builder input).
  - Derive `Debug` and implement `std::fmt::Display` and `std::error::Error` by hand (no `thiserror`; that dep is not approved).
- `ARModel` trait — signature (lifetime parameter shown literally; reproduce verbatim):

```
pub trait ARModel: Send + Sync {
    fn complete<'a>(
        &'a self,
        system_prompt: &'a str,
        messages: &'a [crate::history::Block],
    ) -> std::pin::Pin<Box<dyn futures::Stream<Item = Result<ModelStreamEvent, Error>> + Send + 'a>>;
}
```

The `system_prompt` parameter is added relative to plan.md §"Step 3" because the Agent owns the system prompt (plan.md §"`Agent` configuration") and the model needs it for the Anthropic call. Justified deviation; document with a one-line comment on the trait.
- `DLModel` trait — empty marker trait per plan.md §"`ARModel` and `DLModel`":

```
pub trait DLModel: Send + Sync {}
```

## Module declarations
- `src/model.rs` declares `pub mod anthropic;` (Step 4 fills it; for now `src/model/anthropic.rs` must exist as an empty file — Step 1 should have created it).
- `src/lib.rs` already declares `pub mod model;` (Step 1 work).

## Unit tests
- One test asserting `ModelStreamEvent: Send + Sync` (`fn assert_send_sync<T: Send + Sync>() {}; assert_send_sync::<ModelStreamEvent>();`).
- One test asserting `Error: std::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static`.

## Definition of Done
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, `cargo nextest run` all pass.
- No STYLE_GUIDE.md violations.

## Out of scope
- Any HTTP / SSE / Anthropic-specific code (Step 4)
- Wiring `model::Error` into the crate-root `Error` enum (deferred to a later Bead when all submodule errors land together)
