title: Phase 2 Step 9b: AgentEvent::Idle and client.py tool-call rendering
description:
The current client.py drains events until the first `run_complete` notification and then prompts for the next input. After Phase 2, an agent's response to a single user message can span MULTIPLE model calls (one per tool-call cycle). Each model call emits a `run_complete`. Breaking on the first one cuts the conversation off mid-tool-dispatch.

Fix: introduce a new `AgentEvent::Idle` variant that fires once per outer inbound-loop iteration, AFTER the inner tool-call cycle exits. The client breaks drain on `idle`, not `run_complete`. Also render `tool_call` and `tool_result` BlockComplete events for visibility.

Scope:

1. src/messagebus.rs:
   - Add `AgentEvent::Idle` variant. No fields.
   - Document with a single-line comment: "Emitted once after the agent finishes processing one inbound Block. Subscribers can use this to detect 'agent ready for next input.'"

2. src/agent.rs:
   - In Agent::run, AFTER the inner tool-call cycle exits (i.e. AFTER the `break` from `if pending_tool_calls.is_empty()`), emit `self.bus.publish(self.id, AgentEvent::Idle)` once. Place it INSIDE the outer `while let Some(block) = self.inbound.next().await` loop, AFTER the inner `loop {}`.
   - Cascade fix-up: the existing match on `ModelStreamEvent` in Agent::run is independent of AgentEvent. No change to the model-stream match needed. The new variant only affects AgentEvent producers, not ModelStreamEvent consumers.

3. src/rpc.rs:
   - Extend `AgentEventNotification::from_event` to handle the new variant:
     ```rust
     AgentEvent::Idle => Self {
         agent_id,
         event_type: "idle".to_string(),
         data: serde_json::json!({}),
     },
     ```
   - Add a test `agent_event_notification_idle`: build `AgentEvent::Idle`, call `from_event`, assert `event_type == "idle"` and `data == {}`.

4. Cascade fix-ups (matches on AgentEvent):
   - src/messagebus.rs route() function destructures `AgentEvent::BlockComplete` only with `if let` -- unaffected. Verify by build.
   - src/harness.rs test `subscribe_returns_receiver_seeing_published_event` matches on AgentEvent -- check if any match arm is exhaustive. If exhaustive, add a no-op or assertion for `AgentEvent::Idle`.
   - src/agent.rs tests pattern-match on AgentEvent -- some are exhaustive and need a new arm. For each test, add a no-op `AgentEvent::Idle => {}` arm OR include it in the existing collect set.
   - In the existing test `agent_run_processes_one_user_message`, the count of BlockComplete events is asserted. Verify that test still works -- a single user message should produce: BlockComplete(UserMessage), BlockComplete(AgentMessage), RunComplete, Idle. The drain loop in that test waits for RunComplete; it may now miss Idle, but that's fine because the test asserts on what it captured BEFORE the break. Ensure the test waits for Idle instead of RunComplete (since now Idle is the proper "done" signal). Actually -- the test's drain breaks on `is_run_complete` -- update to break on `is_idle` for forward-compatibility.

5. src/agent.rs tests that wait for TWO RunCompletes (added in bd-1um):
   - Update them to wait for ONE Idle (the Idle event fires once per inbound block, regardless of how many tool cycles). Same drain helper, different terminator.
   - Specifically: `agent_run_dispatches_tool_call_and_re_enters_model`, `agent_run_dispatches_unknown_tool_yields_error_result`, `agent_run_skips_agent_message_when_only_tool_calls` should now drain until Idle.

6. client.py updates:
   - `drain_events`: switch break condition from `run_complete` to `idle`.
   - Add handling for `block_complete` events. Render typed payloads:
     ```python
     elif event_type == "block_complete":
         data = params.get("data", {})
         block_type = data.get("block_type")
         if block_type == "tool_call":
             name = data.get("name", "?")
             args = data.get("arguments", {})
             print(f"\n[tool call: {name}({json.dumps(args)})]", file=sys.stderr)
         elif block_type == "tool_result":
             name = data.get("name", "?")
             result = data.get("result")
             is_error = data.get("is_error", False)
             marker = "tool error" if is_error else "tool result"
             print(f"[{marker}: {name} -> {json.dumps(result)}]", file=sys.stderr)
         # Other block_types (user_message, agent_message, reasoning_trace) are observation-only
         # -- the token_delta stream already renders agent_message content as it streams.
     ```
   - `run_complete` is no longer a terminator. The handler may still print the trailing newline if desired, but the loop continues. Idle is the terminator.
   - Adjust the inline `print(text, end="", flush=True)` for token_delta to remain on stdout (user-facing), keeping tool-call/tool_result on stderr (out-of-band UI).
   - On `idle`, print a trailing newline if not already printed.

Cascade fix-up for client.py drain logic:
- The current code prints a newline ONLY on `run_complete`. After the change, the newline should fire after the model's last text output (which happens before any tool dispatch). Two reasonable fixes:
  a. Print a newline ONCE per token_delta sequence by tracking a flag (`needs_newline = True` when a token_delta is seen; clear on run_complete).
  b. Always print a newline on `idle` (simpler; if no tokens streamed, prints a blank line).
- Use option (b). It's one line of state-management and acceptable trailing whitespace.

Tests:

Rust unit tests already specified above (subscribe_returns_receiver_seeing_published_event update if exhaustive; agent run tests updated to drain until Idle; agent_event_notification_idle in rpc.rs).

No Python test added -- client.py is a smoke utility, not under cargo nextest.

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO:
- Touch any file outside src/messagebus.rs, src/agent.rs, src/rpc.rs, src/test_support.rs (if drain_until helpers need updating), client.py.
- Update DESIGN.md / ARCHITECTURE.md (bd-10).
- Add dependencies.
- Refactor client.py beyond the diff above.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies (Rust or Python -- jsonrpcclient already in use).
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- The `Idle` variant has NO fields. Keep it simple.
- All existing Rust tests must continue to pass (count likely changes by +1 in rpc.rs).

Definition of Done:
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (zero warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` (within 120s)

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl or .beads/last-touched. Use the trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Phase 2 Step 3: Extend ModelStreamEvent with tool-use streaming variants
description:
Extend ModelStreamEvent in src/model.rs with the streaming variants required to surface model-requested tool calls. The model parses these from the provider's stream; the Agent's run loop will consume them in a later bead.

Scope -- src/model.rs only:
- Add three new variants to `pub enum ModelStreamEvent`:
  - `ToolUseStart { id: String, name: String }` -- emitted when the provider begins a tool_use content block. `id` is the provider-issued tool-use identifier; `name` is the tool the model wants to call.
  - `ToolUseDelta { id: String, partial_json: String }` -- emitted for incremental JSON-input deltas of an in-flight tool_use. `partial_json` is a raw JSON fragment; consumers accumulate fragments until the matching `ToolUseComplete` arrives. The `id` lets the Agent disambiguate when multiple tool_uses are interleaved (Anthropic supports this).
  - `ToolUseComplete { id: String, name: String, input: serde_json::Value }` -- emitted when the provider closes a tool_use block. Carries the fully-parsed input JSON (deserialized from the concatenated partial deltas).

- The enum is NOT marked `#[non_exhaustive]` today; do NOT change that. Downstream matchers will be updated by the corresponding integration beads.

Tests (inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block in src/model.rs):
- Construct each new variant by name and verify field access via pattern match.
- Confirm `ModelStreamEvent` remains `Send + Sync` via the existing `assert_send_sync` helper. (Spot-check with one direct call; do not duplicate already-present assertions.)
- For `ToolUseDelta`, verify that two events with different `id`s compare unequal (the enum derives `Clone` and `Debug` but not `PartialEq`; do NOT add a `PartialEq` derive -- just exercise pattern match on `id` to confirm disambiguation by id is possible).

Cascade fix-ups -- the new variants WILL force exhaustive `match` updates in callers:
- src/agent.rs: the run-loop match on `evt?` at the model stream level (look for `match evt? { ModelStreamEvent::ContentDelta { .. } => ... }` near line 182) is exhaustive. After your change it must explicitly mention the three new variants. The MINIMUM-CHANGE policy applies: each new arm is a no-op (the Agent's tool-call wiring lands in a LATER bead). Add:
  ```rust
  ModelStreamEvent::ToolUseStart { .. }
  | ModelStreamEvent::ToolUseDelta { .. }
  | ModelStreamEvent::ToolUseComplete { .. } => {
      // Tool-use dispatch lands in a later bead (Agent run loop).
  }
  ```
- src/model/anthropic.rs: search for any `match` that destructures `ModelStreamEvent` -- if any exists, add the same minimum no-op arm. (The Anthropic adapter currently only PRODUCES ModelStreamEvent values, so no consumer-side match is expected; verify before adding anything.)
- No other production sites should need touching. If clippy or rustc surfaces another exhaustive-match site, add the same minimum arm; do not introduce logic.

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO:
- Wire the Anthropic adapter to emit any of the new variants (separate bead).
- Wire the Agent run loop to dispatch tools (separate bead).
- Touch src/history.rs, src/tool.rs, src/harness.rs, src/messagebus.rs, src/stdio.rs, src/rpc.rs.
- Update DESIGN.md or ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Add dependencies.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies in Cargo.toml.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md: no banner comments, no decorative dividers, no usage examples in doc-comments; comments concise and present-tense.
- Use named-field (struct-style) variants for forward compatibility.

Definition of Done (verify before reporting):
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (zero warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` (within 120s)

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl. Use the trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Split src/config.rs tests module into a sibling tests file
description:
`src/config.rs` is now 600 lines, of which 351 lines (~58%) live in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`. The non-test code is ~250 lines covering the `Config` struct, `LoadArgs`, `assemble_config`, `load_with`, `load`, and the embedded-default constants. The tests module currently carries:

- 3 `assemble_config_*` tests
- 4 `load_with_*` tests
- 2 `embedded_default_topology_*` tests
- Local `MapEnv` and `MockHome` test fixtures (which bd-3dd and the freshly-filed MockHome-hoist bead will move out)

Even after the two fixture-hoist beads land, the test body itself remains ~270 lines — still larger than the non-test code. Future Phase E2 / E3 / F3 work is queued and will add more `load`-pathway integration tests; without a split now, the file will keep ballooning.

The Rust 2018+ "non-mod-rs" module layout used elsewhere in this crate (`src/config/parse.rs`, `src/config/template.rs`, etc.) is the natural shape: move the inline `mod tests` block into a sibling test file.

## Scope (single small commit)

Two acceptable shapes; implementer picks one:

A. Move the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` block from `src/config.rs` into `src/config/tests.rs` and reference it from `src/config.rs` via `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;`. The submodule continues to `use super::*;` (since it sits under `src/config/` while its parent is `src/config.rs`).

B. If shape A is awkward against the current module layout (Rust will treat `src/config/tests.rs` as a submodule of `src/config`, NOT of `src/config.rs` — verify before choosing this), instead split into named feature files: `src/config/tests_assemble.rs`, `src/config/tests_load.rs`, `src/config/tests_embedded_default.rs`, each `pub(super)` and referenced from a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block in `src/config.rs` that simply re-exports.

Shape A is preferred if it works cleanly.

## Out of scope

- Changing any test assertions or body logic.
- Renaming any test function.
- Moving production code out of `src/config.rs`.
- Resolving bd-3dd or the MockHome-hoist bead in the same commit; they are independent and can land in any order.

## Definition of done

- `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- `cargo clippy` passes with no warnings.
- `cargo nextest run` passes; every test currently in `src/config.rs::tests` still runs and passes.
- `src/config.rs` is materially smaller (target: under ~280 lines, all non-test).

title: Phase 1 Step 5: MessageBus and AgentEvent
description:
## Goal
Define the `AgentEvent` outbound wire type and the `MessageBus` trait, plus an `InMemoryMessageBus` implementation that routes one Agent's completed Blocks to another Agent's inbound stream. Phase 1 has a single Agent so routing is trivial; the trait shape must accommodate fan-out and (eventually) cross-process implementations.

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §MessageBus, §Agent (authoritative)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Step 5: MessageBus and AgentEvent", §"Outbound channel", §"Inbound channel"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md`

## Implement in `src/messagebus.rs`
- `pub enum AgentEvent`:
  - `TokenDelta { text: String }`
  - `BlockComplete { block: std::sync::Arc<crate::history::HistoryNode> }`
  - `RunComplete { usage: crate::model::Usage }`
  - Derive `Clone, Debug`.
- `pub enum Error`:
  - `UnknownAgent(crate::history::AgentId)`
  - `Closed` (channel dropped before send/recv could complete)
  - Manual `Display` and `std::error::Error`.
- `pub trait MessageBus: Send + Sync`:
  ```
  fn register(
      &self,
      agent_id: crate::history::AgentId,
  ) -> Result<(
      tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<AgentEvent>,
      tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<crate::history::Block>,
  ), Error>;

  fn outbound(
      &self,
      agent_id: crate::history::AgentId,
  ) -> Result<tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<AgentEvent>, Error>;

  fn subscribe_outbound(
      &self,
      agent_id: crate::history::AgentId,
  ) -> Result<tokio::sync::broadcast::Receiver<AgentEvent>, Error>;

  fn send_inbound(
      &self,
      agent_id: crate::history::AgentId,
      block: crate::history::Block,
  ) -> Result<(), Error>;

  fn route(
      &self,
      from: crate::history::AgentId,
      to: crate::history::AgentId,
  ) -> Result<(), Error>;
  ```
  - `register`: called once per Agent at construction. Returns the broadcast sender it should publish to AND the mpsc receiver it should read inbound Blocks from. The bus retains a clone of the broadcast sender and the mpsc sender keyed by AgentId.
  - `outbound`: hands back a clone of an Agent's outbound broadcast sender (the Agent uses this internally; the Harness uses it to publish externally-originated `BlockComplete`s if needed — Phase 1 does not, but the seam exists).
  - `subscribe_outbound`: hands back a fresh broadcast `Receiver` for any subscriber.
  - `send_inbound`: pushes a `Block` (typically a `UserMessage` from the Harness) onto an Agent's inbound mpsc.
  - `route(from, to)`: spawns a task on the runtime that subscribes to `from`'s outbound, filters for `BlockComplete` carrying an `AgentMessage` block, and pushes each onto `to`'s inbound. Returns immediately. In Phase 1 this is unused (single Agent) but must compile and be tested.
- `pub struct InMemoryMessageBus { /* internal */ }`
  - `pub fn new() -> Self`
  - Implements `MessageBus`.
  - Storage: `std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<AgentId, AgentEntry>>` where `AgentEntry { outbound: broadcast::Sender<AgentEvent>, inbound: mpsc::Sender<Block> }`.
  - Broadcast channel capacity: `1024` (document with a one-line comment that it is lossy by design per plan.md §"Outbound channel"); mpsc capacity: `64` (deliberate; do not unbound).
  - All public methods take `&self` (no `&mut`).

## Tests
- `register_then_send_inbound_delivers_block`: register an agent, call `send_inbound` with a `UserMessage`, recv from the returned receiver, assert content.
- `subscribe_outbound_sees_published_events`: subscribe, then send a `TokenDelta` through the bus's outbound sender clone, assert the subscriber receives it.
- `route_forwards_block_complete_with_agent_message`: register two agents A and B, call `route(A, B)`, then publish a `BlockComplete { block: HistoryNode wrapping an AgentMessage }` through A's outbound sender; assert B's inbound receives the corresponding `Block::AgentMessage`. (Use `tokio::time::timeout` to bound the test at 1s; any failure to receive within that window is a test fail. Do not use sleeps.)
- `unknown_agent_returns_unknown_agent_error`: call `send_inbound` on a never-registered AgentId, assert `Err(Error::UnknownAgent(_))`.

## Wiring
- `src/lib.rs` already declares `pub mod messagebus;` (Step 1 work).
- No change to crate-root `Error` enum in this Bead.

## Definition of Done
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, `cargo nextest run` (incl. these new tests) pass.
- No STYLE_GUIDE.md violations.
- No use of `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.

## Out of scope
- Cross-process bus implementations
- Backpressure policies beyond fixed capacities
- Harness wiring (Step 6 owns the bus)

title: Phase 1 Step 6: Harness, User, lifecycle
description:
## Goal
Implement `User`/`UserId`/`Owner`, the `Harness` struct, `HarnessBuilder`, and the start/shutdown lifecycle. The Harness owns the `MessageBus`, the model registry, and the Agent registry. It exposes `send_to_agent` and `subscribe`.

This Bead does NOT implement the Agent event-loop task — that is Step 7. The Harness must compile and start without it; for now, the "Agent task" the Harness spawns may be a placeholder closure that simply selects on the cancellation token and a drain of the inbound receiver (so the lifecycle is testable end-to-end).

## Reference docs
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §Harness, §Agent (authoritative)
- `blueprint/pristine-phase1-architecture/plan.md` §"Step 6: Harness, User, lifecycle"
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md`

## Implement in `src/user.rs`
- Do NOT define `UserId` here. Per bd-2ix, the canonical home for both `UserId` and `AgentId` is `src/history.rs` (they are tightly coupled to `Block` variants and define the data model). Re-export it from `src/user.rs`:
  ```
  pub use crate::history::UserId;
  ```
- `pub struct User { id: UserId }` — fields private.
- `impl User`:
  - `pub fn new() -> Self` — generates a random `UserId::new()`.
  - `pub fn id(&self) -> UserId`
- `impl Default for User { fn default() -> Self { Self::new() } }`

## Implement in `src/harness.rs`

### Dependency approval
**`tokio-util = { version = "0.7", features = ["rt"] }` is APPROVED for this Bead.** Add it to `Cargo.toml`'s `[dependencies]` block. Use `tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken` for cooperative shutdown. Rationale: hand-rolling shutdown via `tokio::sync::Notify` has known foot-guns; `CancellationToken` is the canonical solution.

This is the ONLY new dependency authorized for Step 6.

### Types

**`ModelId`**
```
pub struct ModelId(String);
```
Derive `Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash`. Provide `pub fn new(name: impl Into<String>) -> Self`.

**`PendingAgent`** — the builder accumulates this struct, NOT a real `Agent` (the real `Agent` lands in Step 7). Define it here for now; Step 7 will replace it as needed.
```
pub struct PendingAgent {
    pub id: crate::history::AgentId,
    pub system_prompt: String,
    pub model_id: ModelId,
}
```
Public fields are acceptable here because this is a plain data carrier consumed by the builder. Derive `Clone, Debug`.

**`Harness`** — fields private:
- `models: HashMap<ModelId, Arc<dyn crate::model::ARModel>>`
- `agents: HashMap<crate::history::AgentId, AgentHandle>` where `AgentHandle` is a private struct holding the spawned task's `JoinHandle<Result<(), Error>>` AND the broadcast `Sender<AgentEvent>` clone (so `subscribe` can hand out fresh receivers without going through the bus every time — though going through the bus is fine too)
- `bus: Arc<crate::messagebus::InMemoryMessageBus>`
- `owner: crate::user::User`
- `cancel: tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken`
- `pending: Vec<PendingAgent>` — populated by `HarnessBuilder::build()`; consumed by `Harness::start()`

**`HarnessBuilder`**:
- `pub fn new() -> Self` — empty registries
- `pub fn add_model(self, id: ModelId, model: Arc<dyn ARModel>) -> Self`
- `pub fn add_agent(self, agent: PendingAgent) -> Self`
- `pub fn build(self) -> Result<Harness, Error>` — validates that every `PendingAgent::model_id` exists in `models`; returns `Err(Error::UnknownModel(id))` if not. Instantiates the `User` (the Owner) here.
- `impl Default for HarnessBuilder { fn default() -> Self { Self::new() } }`

### Lifecycle methods on `Harness`
- `pub fn owner_id(&self) -> crate::user::UserId`
- `pub fn start(&mut self) -> Result<(), Error>` — for each `PendingAgent`:
  1. Register it with the bus to obtain its outbound `Sender<AgentEvent>` and inbound `Receiver<Block>`.
  2. Spawn a `tokio::task` that is the **PLACEHOLDER** for the real Agent loop (Step 7 replaces this). The placeholder body:
     ```rust
     async move {
         loop {
             tokio::select! {
                 _ = cancel.cancelled() => return Ok::<(), Error>(()),
                 maybe = inbound.recv() => {
                     if maybe.is_none() { return Ok(()); }
                     // discard the block in Phase 1 Step 6 placeholder
                 }
             }
         }
     }
     ```
  3. Store the `JoinHandle` and the outbound `Sender` clone in `AgentHandle` keyed by the AgentId.

  `start` returns `Ok(())` once all spawns complete; it does NOT await them.

- `pub async fn run_until_shutdown(self) -> Result<(), Error>` — consumes `self`; awaits every spawned `JoinHandle` in turn. Returns the first error encountered (or `Ok(())` if all succeeded). Lifecycle shape: `start()` is synchronous; `run_until_shutdown()` is the awaitable hold-point. This is a justified deviation from plan.md's `start() -> JoinHandle`. Document with one terse comment.

- `pub fn shutdown(&self)` — `self.cancel.cancel()`. Idempotent (cancellation tokens are idempotent by design).

- `pub fn send_to_agent(&self, agent_id: AgentId, from: UserId, content: String) -> Result<(), Error>`:
  - Build `Block::UserMessage { from, content, timestamp: SystemTime::now() }`.
  - Forward to `bus.send_inbound(agent_id, block)`.
  - Map `messagebus::Error` via `From`.

- `pub fn subscribe(&self, agent_id: AgentId) -> Result<broadcast::Receiver<AgentEvent>, Error>`:
  - Proxy to `bus.subscribe_outbound(agent_id)`.

### `Error` enum
```
pub enum Error {
    UnknownModel(ModelId),
    UnknownAgent(crate::history::AgentId),
    Lifecycle(String),
    Bus(crate::messagebus::Error),
    Join(String),
}
```
Derive `Debug`. Manual `Display` + `std::error::Error`. `source()` returns `Some(&bus_err)` for `Bus`, `None` otherwise. `From<messagebus::Error> for Error` mapping to `Bus(...)`. `From<tokio::task::JoinError> for Error` mapping to `Join(err.to_string())`.

### Tests
- `builder_rejects_agent_with_unknown_model` — assert `Err(UnknownModel(_))`.
- `start_then_shutdown_exits_cleanly` — bound the test with `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), ...)`. Build a Harness with one Agent and a `StubArModel` (test-local). `start()`. `shutdown()`. `run_until_shutdown().await` returns `Ok(())`.
- `send_to_agent_routes_through_bus` — register an Agent. Start. Subscribe to ITS outbound (just to keep the broadcast alive — the placeholder loop discards inbound, so we can't directly observe inbound delivery via subscribe). Instead, expose `bus()` as `pub(crate)` ONLY for tests, OR construct a parallel `InMemoryMessageBus` reference for the test. Simplest: add a `#[cfg(test)] pub fn bus(&self) -> &Arc<InMemoryMessageBus>` accessor and use it from the test to register a second listener. Document this with one terse `#[cfg(test)]` comment.
- `subscribe_returns_receiver_seeing_published_event` — subscribe to an Agent's outbound, then publish a `TokenDelta` via the bus's outbound sender; subscriber receives it within `timeout(1s, ...)`.

`StubArModel` lives in the test module returning an empty `Stream` (use `futures::stream::empty()` boxed and pinned).

### Style
- No banner comments. No `unwrap`/`expect` outside tests. No `plan.md`/bead-ID references in source.

## Files you may NOT touch
- `src/agent.rs` (Step 7)
- `src/bin/pristine.rs` (Step 8)
- `src/history.rs`, `src/model.rs`, `src/model/anthropic.rs`, `src/messagebus.rs`, `src/lib.rs` (no edits required)
- Document files
- `.beads/`

## Files you MAY touch
- `src/harness.rs` (full implementation)
- `src/user.rs` (replace stub with User + UserId re-export)
- `Cargo.toml` (only to add `tokio-util = { version = "0.7", features = ["rt"] }`)

## Verification
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` — expect 21 tests pass (17 prior + 4 new) + 1 ignored

## Out of scope
- Real Agent event-loop (Step 7)
- Multi-model adaptive-reasoning dispatch
- Persistence

title: Remove unused ConfigError::ExtraneousAlias variant
description:
## Problem

`ConfigError::ExtraneousAlias { alias: String }` is declared in `src/config/error.rs:28` with a full `Display` impl (lines 66-71) and `Error::source` membership (line 101). It is never constructed anywhere in the codebase:

```
$ grep -rn 'ExtraneousAlias' src/ tests/
src/config/error.rs:28:    ExtraneousAlias { alias: String },
src/config/error.rs:66:            ConfigError::ExtraneousAlias { alias } => {
src/config/error.rs:101:            | ConfigError::ExtraneousAlias { .. }
```

The semantic intent was clarified during C1 (`bd-242`): `src/config/resolve.rs:7-10` explicitly states 'extra aliases declared in auth but unused by topology are intentionally not errors (auth files are shared across topologies)', and the regression test `extra_alias_is_not_an_error` (`src/config/resolve.rs:158`) pins that behavior. This is distinct from `UnknownProvider` and `MissingHome`, which are also currently unused but reserved for D1/D2/E2 by design. Those should NOT be removed in this bead.

## Scope (single commit)

1. Delete the `ExtraneousAlias { alias: String }` arm from `ConfigError` in `src/config/error.rs` (line 25-28 area).
2. Delete the `Display` arm for `ExtraneousAlias` in the same file (lines 66-71).
3. Delete the `ConfigError::ExtraneousAlias { .. }` token from the catch-all source() match arm (line 101).

## Out of scope

- Removing `UnknownProvider` or `MissingHome` (both are reserved for upcoming beads — confirmed by inspection of the open queue).
- Any other variant cleanup.
- Reworking `ConfigErrors` aggregate API.

## Definition of done

- `cargo clippy` passes with no warnings.
- `cargo nextest run` passes (no test references `ExtraneousAlias` today).
- The variant is not present in any `grep -rn 'ExtraneousAlias' src/ tests/` output.

title: Redesign Harness lifecycle for &mut consistency and per-agent fault isolation
description:
## Goal
The Phase 1 Harness lifecycle has three different ownership models for the related methods (`start(&mut)`, `run_until_shutdown(self)`, `shutdown(&self)`). This was flagged in the conceptual integrity review as "the codebase's largest single conceptual fracture" and as actively hostile to the multi-Agent goal in `ARCHITECTURE.md`. Make the lifecycle uniform: every method that mutates lifecycle state takes `&mut self`. Interaction methods (`send_to_agent`, `subscribe`, `owner_id`) keep `&self`. Drop `run_until_shutdown(self)` consumption. Replace it with `join(&mut self)`.

Also remove the cancel-on-agent-error cascade. Agent failures must be isolated: one Agent dying does NOT cancel the others. Per-agent failure is communicated via the existing `AgentEvent::Error` event; the caller decides what to do.

Update `ARCHITECTURE.md §Harness` to reflect the new lifecycle and the per-agent-fault-isolation policy.

## Reference
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` §Harness (this file is authoritative; you ARE updating it as part of this Bead)
- `src/harness.rs` — the lifecycle methods
- `src/lib.rs::run_async` — the binary's drive pattern
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md`

## Required changes

### 1. `src/harness.rs` — public lifecycle API

Current signatures:
- `pub fn start(&mut self) -> Result<(), Error>`
- `pub async fn run_until_shutdown(self) -> Result<(), Error>`  ← REMOVE
- `pub fn shutdown(&self)`  ← CHANGE TO `&mut self`
- `pub fn send_to_agent(&self, AgentId, UserId, String) -> Result<(), Error>`  ← unchanged
- `pub fn subscribe(&self, AgentId) -> Result<broadcast::Receiver<AgentEvent>, Error>`  ← unchanged
- `pub fn owner_id(&self) -> UserId`  ← unchanged

New signatures:
- `pub fn start(&mut self) -> Result<(), Error>`
- `pub async fn join(&mut self) -> Result<(), Error>` — awaits every spawned task's `JoinHandle`. The handles are stored on `self.agents` (already populated by `start`). This method DRAINS them — after it returns, the agent registry is empty and subsequent `join()` calls return `Ok(())` immediately (idempotent).
- `pub fn shutdown(&mut self)` — fires the cancellation token. Idempotent (cancellation tokens already are).
- `pub fn send_to_agent(&self, ...)` — unchanged
- `pub fn subscribe(&self, ...)` — unchanged
- `pub fn owner_id(&self) -> UserId` — unchanged

Implementation notes for `join`:
- Take `&mut self`. The future borrows `self.agents` mutably across the await.
- Iterate (e.g., via `let handles = std::mem::take(&mut self.agents);` then drain) so the `&mut` borrow is released before each `.await`. Alternative: `while let Some((_, handle)) = self.agents.drain().next()` — but `drain()` returns an iterator, not a stream, so accumulate the handles first.
- Concrete pattern:
  ```rust
  let mut handles: Vec<_> = std::mem::take(&mut self.agents)
      .into_values()
      .map(|h| h.handle)
      .collect();
  let mut first_err: Option<Error> = None;
  for handle in handles.drain(..) {
      match handle.await {
          Ok(Ok(())) => {}
          Ok(Err(e)) if first_err.is_none() => first_err = Some(e),
          Ok(Err(_)) => {} // subsequent errors are swallowed; first wins
          Err(join_err) if first_err.is_none() => first_err = Some(Error::from(join_err)),
          Err(_) => {}
      }
  }
  match first_err {
      Some(e) => Err(e),
      None => Ok(()),
  }
  ```
  Note: this drops the broadcast `Sender` clones owned by `AgentHandle` as each one is moved out. That's actually what we want — once all agents have exited, subscribers see `Closed` cleanly.

The `AgentHandle` struct stays as-is (no field-level changes); just make sure its `handle: JoinHandle<Result<(), Error>>` is the only field move you take.

### 2. `src/harness.rs` — remove cancel-on-agent-error cascade

In `Harness::start`'s spawn body (around line 175 per the prior code), the current pattern is:
```rust
if let Err(ref e) = res {
    let _ = outbound_for_emit.send(AgentEvent::Error { message: e.to_string() });
    cancel_for_task.cancel();   // <-- REMOVE THIS LINE
}
res
```

Drop ONLY the `cancel_for_task.cancel();` line. Keep the `AgentEvent::Error` emission — it's the per-agent error signal. The Agent task then exits naturally, the spawned future resolves with `Err`, the JoinHandle resolves, and `join()` collects the error.

Multi-agent semantics: when one agent dies, ONLY its task exits. Other agents continue. The caller observes the error via `AgentEvent::Error` on the failing agent's outbound stream and decides whether to call `shutdown()`.

### 3. `src/lib.rs::run_async` — update the binary

The binary currently calls `harness.run_until_shutdown().await?` after the recv loop. Replace with `harness.join().await?`. No other changes needed in `run_async` — the recv loop, the `RunComplete` counter, the `AgentEvent::Error` arm, the `shutdown()` call all keep working.

### 4. Tests in `src/harness.rs`

- `start_then_shutdown_exits_cleanly` — was bounded with `timeout`. Update to use `join` instead of `run_until_shutdown`. Still bounded.
- `harness_emits_error_event_when_agent_dies` — currently asserts `run_until_shutdown` does not hang after an agent dies. Update to use `join`. The test should ALSO verify (new behavior) that `shutdown()` is NOT required to be called for `join()` to return when all agents have already exited. Specifically: with one agent that errors, after the binary receives `AgentEvent::Error`, calling `harness.join().await` should complete WITHOUT needing a prior `shutdown()`, because the agent task has already finished. Add this assertion.

  Then keep the existing assertion that `harness.shutdown(); harness.join().await` also completes cleanly (with shutdown being effectively a no-op since the agent is already dead).
- `send_to_agent_routes_through_bus` — unaffected.
- `subscribe_returns_receiver_seeing_published_event` — unaffected.
- `builder_rejects_agent_with_unknown_model` — unaffected.

Add ONE new test:
- `multi_agent_failure_isolation` — register TWO agents, A and B. Agent A's `StubArModel` yields a single `Err`. Agent B's `StubArModel` returns `empty()` (so the agent's `complete` is never even invoked because we won't send B a message). Start, subscribe to BOTH. `send_to_agent(A, owner, ...)`. Within `timeout(Duration::from_secs(2), ...)`, assert agent A's subscriber receives `AgentEvent::Error`. Then assert agent B's task is still running (e.g., its `JoinHandle` is not yet finished — you may need to expose a `#[cfg(test)]` helper, or simply call `shutdown()` and `join()` and assert both agents' join results, expecting A=Err and B=Ok). Bound the test with timeout.

### 5. `ARCHITECTURE.md` — §Harness

Replace the existing lifecycle paragraph (currently ending with "...not usable after shutdown.") with:

```markdown
The Harness has an explicit lifecycle:

- `start(&mut self) -> Result<(), Error>` synchronously spawns the per-Agent tasks and the MessageBus routing tasks. Returns once spawning is complete.
- `shutdown(&mut self)` signals cooperative termination by firing a cancellation token that every spawned task observes. Idempotent.
- `join(&mut self) -> impl Future<Output = Result<(), Error>>` is async; it awaits every spawned task to completion and returns the first error encountered, or `Ok(())` if all tasks exited cleanly. Idempotent: after `join` returns, the Agent registry is empty and a subsequent `join` returns `Ok(())` immediately.
- `send_to_agent(&self, ...)` and `subscribe(&self, ...)` interact with the running Harness via `&self`, so they can be called freely between `start` and `join`. They cannot be called concurrently with `join` itself because `join` holds `&mut self`.

**Per-Agent fault isolation.** When an Agent's `run` task exits with an `Err` (e.g., an underlying Model returns an error), the Harness emits `AgentEvent::Error { message }` on that Agent's outbound stream so subscribers can detect the failure and react. Other Agents continue running. The Harness does NOT cascade-cancel on a single Agent's failure; the policy of when to call `shutdown()` is the caller's responsibility. This isolation is required to support the architectural goal of "multiple parallel agents running within one process."
```

Do NOT change any other paragraph in `ARCHITECTURE.md`. Do NOT touch `STYLE_GUIDE.md`, `README.md`, or `blueprint/`.

### 6. Documentation comments on the methods
Add ONE terse line of documentation above each of `start`, `join`, and `shutdown` in `src/harness.rs`, mirroring the architecture description. Do NOT cross-reference `ARCHITECTURE.md` or any other document. One line per method.

### 7. Style
- No banner comments.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- No `plan.md`/`bd-` references in source comments.

## Files you MAY modify
- `src/harness.rs`
- `src/lib.rs`
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` (the §Harness paragraph only — strict scope)

## Files you may NOT touch
- `src/agent.rs`, `src/model.rs`, `src/model/anthropic.rs`, `src/history.rs`, `src/user.rs`, `src/messagebus.rs`, `src/test_support.rs`
- `src/bin/pristine.rs`, `src/bin/1p.rs`
- `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md`, `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `TESTING.md`
- `blueprint/`
- `.beads/`, `tests/`

## Verification
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo nextest run` — expect 28 tests pass (27 prior + 1 new) + 1 skipped. The two updated tests (`start_then_shutdown_exits_cleanly`, `harness_emits_error_event_when_agent_dies`) still pass with their updated assertions.
- `cargo build` — zero warnings
- Manual smoke test (Coordinator runs): `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=fakedeadbeef cargo run --bin 1p -- run 2>&1` exits non-zero within ~10s with an `agent error:` message (same behavior as before, since the binary's recv loop still detects `AgentEvent::Error`).
- `grep -rn 'plan.md\|bd-\|run_until_shutdown' src/` returns zero matches (the old method name is fully removed).

## Out of scope
- The Block abstraction redesign (separate Bead under discussion)
- Any change to the MessageBus trait surface
- Any change to error type layering inside `harness::Error`
- The `#[cfg(test)] pub fn bus()` accessor (separate cleanup)

title: Consolidate EchoTool test fixture into test_support
description:
## Problem

Three near-identical `EchoTool` implementations have accumulated in test modules across the crate:

1. `src/tool.rs::tests::EchoTool` (parametric `new(name: &str)`)
2. `src/harness.rs::tests::EchoTool` (added in bd-2i9, fixed name "echo")
3. `src/agent.rs::tests::EchoTool` (added in bd-2t6, fixed name "echo")

Items 2 and 3 are byte-identical (~30 lines each: struct, `new`, four trait methods). Item 1 differs only in that it accepts a custom name. The third occurrence landed in bd-2t6 by copy-paste from bd-2i9.

This is the accretion pattern that compounds. When bd-8 (demo tool + binary wiring) lands it is highly likely to add a fourth occurrence in a bin-level test or in `src/lib.rs` tests, because the obvious move when writing a new test is to copy the existing fixture.

## Required change

Move `EchoTool` into `src/test_support.rs` as a shared fixture exposed `pub(crate)`:

- Struct + `new(name: &str)` constructor (use the parametric form from tool.rs).
- `Tool` trait impl identical to existing copies.

Then delete the three inline copies. Tests that used the fixed-name variant just call `EchoTool::new("echo")`.

## Acceptance

- `cargo fmt --check` passes.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` passes.
- `cargo nextest run` shows the same test count (80) passing.
- `grep -rn 'struct EchoTool' src/` returns exactly one hit (in `test_support.rs`).

## Out of scope

- Do NOT promote `EchoTool` to `pub`. `test_support.rs` is gated behind `#[cfg(test)] mod test_support;`; `pub(crate)` items there are correctly scoped.
- Do NOT touch `StubArModel`.
- Do NOT add new tool fixtures (e.g., a failing tool). If bd-8 needs a different fixture, it can add one in the same file.

title: Introduce ModelInput; remove Block from Model trait
Phase 2 Step 9a: Expand BlockComplete JSON-RPC notification to carry per-block data
description:
The current AgentEventNotification in src/rpc.rs renders a BlockComplete event as `{ "type": "block_complete", "data": {} }` -- with EMPTY data. Subscribers cannot tell whether the block is a UserMessage, ToolCall, ToolResult, AgentMessage, or ReasoningTrace. This was an acceptable pre-Phase-2 gap (only AgentMessage and UserMessage blocks ever flowed), but is now material: the Python client (bd-9b) needs to render tool activity, and the server is the only place that knows the Block shape.

Scope -- src/rpc.rs only:

1. Extend `AgentEventNotification::from_event` so that the BlockComplete branch produces a typed payload:

```rust
AgentEvent::BlockComplete { block } => Self {
    agent_id,
    event_type: "block_complete".to_string(),
    data: block_to_value(block.block()),
},
```

Add a free function:

```rust
fn block_to_value(block: &Block) -> serde_json::Value {
    match block {
        Block::UserMessage { from, content, .. } => serde_json::json!({
            "block_type": "user_message",
            "from": from,
            "content": content,
        }),
        Block::AgentMessage { from, content, .. } => serde_json::json!({
            "block_type": "agent_message",
            "from": from,
            "content": content,
        }),
        Block::ToolCall { id, name, arguments, .. } => serde_json::json!({
            "block_type": "tool_call",
            "id": id,
            "name": name,
            "arguments": arguments,
        }),
        Block::ToolResult { tool_use_id, name, result, is_error, .. } => serde_json::json!({
            "block_type": "tool_result",
            "tool_use_id": tool_use_id,
            "name": name,
            "result": result,
            "is_error": is_error,
        }),
        Block::ReasoningTrace { content, .. } => serde_json::json!({
            "block_type": "reasoning_trace",
            "content": content,
        }),
    }
}
```

Notes:
- `timestamp` field is intentionally NOT serialized in this bead. It is SystemTime; serializing it requires an explicit format choice. Defer to a later observability bead.
- The discriminator field is named `block_type` (not `type`) to avoid collision with the outer notification's `type` field. The outer notification uses `event_type` serialized as `"type"`; the inner data uses `block_type` so a downstream consumer sees:
  ```json
  {"type": "block_complete", "data": {"block_type": "tool_call", ...}}
  ```

2. Tests in src/rpc.rs `mod tests`:

The existing `agent_event_notification_block_complete` test currently only checks `event_type == "block_complete"` and does not inspect data. Replace and augment.

Add five tests (one per Block variant), each constructing a HistoryNode with the variant, calling `AgentEventNotification::from_event(agent_id, &AgentEvent::BlockComplete { block: Arc<HistoryNode> })`, and asserting the resulting `data` payload:

- `block_complete_notification_user_message_shape`: assert data.block_type == "user_message", data.from is a UUID string, data.content matches.
- `block_complete_notification_agent_message_shape`: same pattern.
- `block_complete_notification_tool_call_shape`: id, name, arguments round-trip; block_type == "tool_call".
- `block_complete_notification_tool_result_shape`: tool_use_id, name, result, is_error round-trip; block_type == "tool_result".
- `block_complete_notification_reasoning_trace_shape`: content round-trip; block_type == "reasoning_trace".

The OLD `agent_event_notification_block_complete` test can either be DELETED (its assertions are subsumed by the new tests) OR kept and adjusted to confirm only that `event_type == "block_complete"`. Pick whichever yields the smallest diff; report your choice.

Out of scope -- DO NOT DO:
- Touch client.py (bd-9b will use the new payload).
- Add Block::timestamp serialization (out of scope; that's an observability bead).
- Touch any file other than src/rpc.rs.
- Update DESIGN.md / ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Add dependencies.

Constraints:
- No new dependencies.
- No `unwrap`/`expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md compliance.
- The `block_to_value` function is a private free function within src/rpc.rs (not `pub`).
- AgentId and UserId already derive Serialize; they will JSON-serialize as their UUID string representation. Confirm by reading the existing derives in src/history.rs.

Definition of Done:
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (zero warnings)
- `cargo nextest run` (within 120s, test count = 89 = 85 prior + 5 new minus 1 old replaced; or 90 if you kept the old test).

Commit policy: one atomic commit. Do NOT modify .beads/issues.jsonl or .beads/last-touched. Use the trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>`.

title: Phase B2: TOML deserialization into typed structs
description:
Plan: blueprint/configuration-file/plan-configuration-file.md (Phase B2).

Add functions to src/config that read a file path and deserialize into TopologyConfig / AuthConfig using toml::from_str. No templating, no resolution. Errors from toml::de::Error map into ConfigError::TomlParse with line/col info (toml::de::Error exposes a span).

Constraints:
- Two distinct entry points: load_topology_str(&str) and load_auth_str(&str). String-based to allow testing without file IO. File IO (read_to_string) is a thin wrapper around the str-based variant.
- Failures return ConfigError, not anyhow.
- Unit tests for: valid TOML round-trip into TopologyConfig and AuthConfig; deny_unknown_fields rejection; invalid TOML with garbled syntax returns ConfigError::TomlParse.

Files to read: src/config/ (from B1), and the plan for the expected TOML shape.
